  • QA managers can quickly identify abnormal activity related to any item so they can take appropriate steps to mitigate issues.
  • Production managers can easily identify delays or bottlenecks in the manufacturing process.
  • Field service workers can quickly understand the lifecycle of any item to verify it and make sure the item qualifies for repair or replacement.

The activities page in Supply Chain Management includes a Copilot-generated summary of recent activities and a full list of activity details. Copilot provides an activity summary that highlights the related item's status, plus any other information. From there, users can drill down into the activity to find related details. The summary for each activity provides instant access to key information such as location, time, items, workers, vendors, and more.
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts

results.Feature name: Track time-series inventory in Inventory Visibility
results.Business value: External systems integrated with Dynamics 365 Supply Chain Management can now access accurate and up-to-date inventory availability information. With this capability, users can more efficiently and confidently promise sales orders, search for spare parts, and view material availability.
results.Feature details:
The Inventory Visibility service now enables external systems to query Supply Chain Management for inventory changes up to 180 days in the future. As a result, users working in an external system can view future inventory availability details and make accurate projections for when orders can be delivered.

This feature enables the Inventory Visibility service to import information about planned inbound and outbound inventory changes along with the related dates. Available-to-promise (ATP) functionality in Inventory Visibility can query these time-series inventory changes and calculate your omnichannel ATP. External systems can query and retrieve this information from Inventory Visibility in near real time by calling its API.
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts

results.Feature name: Add transactions when recalculating weighted average
results.Business value: Get more control over the recalculation process when you choose to exclude or include specific types of transactions. This control helps ensure report accuracy and regulation compliance for some regions. By streamlining the recalculation process, this feature saves time and increases efficiency. It reduces the need for manual intervention.
results.Feature details:
When recalculating the weighted average, you can now choose to include transactions that aren't yet financially updated. This feature:

  • Adds an option for item model groups.
  • Includes transactions without financial closing.
  • Applies only during recalculations and doesn't affect inventory closing.
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts

results.Feature name: Enhance discrete manufacturing costing
results.Business value: Many international discrete manufacturing companies are required to implement parallel inventory accounting practices for standard cost (based on a single document) using multiple currencies or cost calculation methods. In addition, discrete manufacturers need a comprehensive and practical solution to cover as many business scenarios as possible. Interactive and informative Power BI report templates provide vital evidence for management to analyze and use to make strategic and tactical changes in the future.
results.Feature details:
This enhancement for the Global Inventory Accounting Add-in for Dynamics 365 Supply Chain Management enables discrete manufacturing companies to perform accurate parallel inventory accounting practices for standard cost (based on a single document) using multiple configurations. This release enables you to:

  • Gain an improved onboarding experience that helps you configure and set up Global Inventory Accounting.
  • Track and record discrete manufacturing costs for related business documents such as sub-production orders.
  • Process returns for trade transactions that support certain marking documents.
  • Create enhanced power BI reports that provide a comprehensive view covering both trading and manufacturing cost transactions.

This feature is turned on by default because it provides an essential enhancement to existing functionality. It's listed as Evaluate costs in discrete manufacturing using standard cost in the Feature management workspace, where you can turn it off if needed.
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts

results.Feature name: Evaluate discrete manufacturing costs using standard cost
results.Business value: Many international discrete manufacturing companies are under pressure to comply with both local generally accepted accounting practices and statutory accounting principles (inventory financial reporting standards), even when these conflict with one another. In addition, companies or their subsidiaries may need to account for inventory cost using both a local, fluctuating currency and a solid second currency for statutory compliance. Finally, companies may need to use the standard cost valuation method internally plus another valuation method required by their local country or region. Manufacturing companies can now use the Global Inventory Accounting Add-in for Microsoft Dynamics 365 Supply Chain Management to perform parallel inventory accounting practices using standard cost (based on a single document).
results.Feature details:
This enhancement for the Global Inventory Accounting Add-in enables discrete manufacturing companies to perform parallel inventory accounting practices using standard cost (based on a single document) and multiple currencies. This enables you to:

  • Build three categories of manufacturing expenses (materials, labor, and overhead) by setting up cost element policies.
  • Gain an accurate understanding of cost inflow and outflow processes. Visible posting policies and rules establish the foundation for enhanced transparency.
  • Track and record discrete manufacturing costs by applying standard cost calculations to production documents (including picking lists, route cards, job cards, and report-as-finished journals).
  • Query for operational events and measurements related to production orders to track and trace the calculation basis. You can also query for events and measurements within Global Inventory Accounting for calculated results in different ledgers.
  • Analyze costing results and make management decisions using visual, interactive, and informative Power BI reports embedded into the system.

The collection of features included in this release supports several typical use cases. In future releases, we will continue to expand this functionality to further enhance support for discrete manufacturers.
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts

results.Feature name: Archive historical inventory journal records
results.Business value:
Over time, companies generate and store a large volume of inventory journal records. Although day-to-day operations don't require these records, you might need them for historical reporting, auditing, machine learning, and other purposes. Keeping a large volume of historical inventory journal records in your day-to-day working environment increases storage costs and affects system performance and usability.

With this feature, you can use the archival framework to archive historical inventory journal data by using rules. The framework removes the records from your day-to-day working environment and stores them in your Dataverse-managed data lake. This approach improves system performance and lowers operating costs while keeping your historical inventory journal records available as read-only data when you need them.
results.Feature details: Use the data archival solution in Microsoft finance and operations apps to archive your historical inventory journal records. Set up logical rules to control when and how to archive records from your day-to-day Supply Chain Management database to your Dataverse-managed data lake. The data stays available for historical reporting, auditing, machine learning, legal claims, and other purposes.
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts

results.Feature name: Archive inventory transactions
results.Business value:
Over time, your company generates and stores a large volume of inventory transaction records. Although day-to-day operations don't require these records, you might need them for historical reporting, auditing, machine learning, and other purposes. Keeping a large volume of historical inventory transaction records in your day-to-day working environment increases storage costs and affects system performance and usability.

With this feature, you can use the archival framework to archive historical inventory transaction data by using rules. The framework removes the archived records from your day-to-day working environment and stores them in your Dataverse-managed data lake. This approach improves system performance and lowers operating costs while keeping your historical inventory transaction records available as read-only data when you need them.
results.Feature details: Use the data archival solution in Microsoft finance and operations apps to archive your historical inventory transaction records. Administrators set up logical rules that control when and how to archive records from the day-to-day Supply Chain Management database to the Dataverse-managed data lake. The data stays available for historical reporting, auditing, machine learning, legal claims, and other purposes.
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts

results.Feature name: Archive sales orders
results.Business value:
Over time, your company typically generates and stores a large volume of sales orders and sales order lines. Although day-to-day operations don't require these records, you might need them for historical reporting, auditing, machine learning, legal claims, and other purposes. Keeping a large volume of historical sales orders and lines in your day-to-day working environment increases storage costs and affects system performance and usability.

With this feature, you can use the archival framework to archive historical sales orders and lines based on rules. The framework removes the archived records from your day-to-day working environment and stores them in your Dataverse-managed data lake. This approach improves system performance and lowers operating costs while keeping your historical sales records available as read-only data when you need them.
results.Feature details: Use the data archival solution in Microsoft finance and operations apps to archive your historical sales orders and sales order lines. Administrators set up logical rules that control when and how to archive sales orders and sales order lines from the day-to-day Supply Chain Management database to the Dataverse-managed data lake. The data stays available for historical reporting, auditing, machine learning, legal claims, and other purposes.
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts

results.Feature name: Allow net price in Unified pricing management
results.Business value: This feature enhances the Unified pricing management module by adding support for negotiated final prices in trade agreements. With this feature, you can define sales prices directly, with or without price adjustments, and explicitly prevent the application of extra discounts. This functionality ensures accurate discount-exempt pricing that aligns with your contractual or wholesale pricing needs. In some situations, you negotiate a trade agreement price with customers and treat that price as an exclusive final price without discounts. Unified pricing management lets you set sales prices as negotiated net prices without considering discounts.
results.Feature details:
This feature enhances the Unified pricing management module sales trade agreement pricing experience by allowing pricing administrators to define:

  • A final exclusive net price that reflects the outcome of negotiation.
  • Inclusion of price adjustments, such as percentage increases or decreases.
  • Prevention of promotional or system-applied discounts from further affecting the transaction price.

The feature supports the following key use cases:

  • B2B or wholesale pricing where negotiated prices honor without promotional interference.
  • Long-term contracts that require strict adherence to fixed pricing, which excludes further discounts.

This functionality is especially relevant in B2B, channel sales, and regulated pricing models, where discount logic doesn't alter the agreed price.

This feature provides the following key capability:

  • Prevent discount – Choose to prevent any extra discount.
  • Allow price adjustment – Choose whether to apply adjustments, such as a markup or markdown, to the sales price.
  • Net price definition – Set Allow price adjustment to No and Prevent discount to Yes to define the trade agreement sales price as a net (final, exclusive) price that doesn't allow further discounting.
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts

results.Feature name: Enhance supply chain traceability with tracking attributes
results.Business value: Tracking attributes enhance supply chain traceability. They help stakeholders, such as quality managers, sales managers, and production managers, analyze deviations by using captured data. Stakeholders can now get lists of product serial numbers or batch numbers based on business activities and tracking attribute data.
results.Feature details:
You can configure data collection and assign it to specific activity types and codes to store measurements and attributes for tracking and tracing. With this configuration, you can monitor and manage various activities within the supply chain.

The system also enables you to retrieve all measurements and attributes related to each serial number, batch number, asset number, or lot number. This retrieval process ensures that you can access all relevant data for analysis and decision-making.

You can perform a where-used search to retrieve impacted products based on specific measurements and attributes. For example, you can retrieve all impacted VIN numbers for Model Y vehicles that you produced between August 23, 2024, and September 25, 2024, where the painting temperature was below 80 degrees. This capability helps you identify and address potential issues in the production process.
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts

results.Feature name: Enhance vendor rebate management
results.Business value: Vendor rebate management clerks can resubmit and edit vendor rebate deals. Source transaction vendors can reconcile their company output, such as accounts payable invoices, which adds flexibility.
results.Feature details:
The key capabilities that provide approval and reconciliation flexibility include:

  • A new rebate management workflow that enables a resubmission process for vendors.
  • Enhanced posting profiles that allow you to reconcile transactions according to the purchased-from vendor instead of using fixed, predefined vendors.
  • Improved rebate output for vendor invoices that consolidates multiple ledger lines for the same vendor into a single vendor line with several ledger lines.
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts

results.Feature name: Enable quality control for goods-in-transit orders
results.Business value: When you add quality control to the goods-in-transit process in Dynamics 365, you transform supply chain operations. This integration makes sure that products go through strict quality checks during transit, so the warehouse always receives high-quality goods.
results.Feature details:
Quality orders in Supply Chain Management now support goods-in-transit orders as a reference type. With this support, you can start the quality control process for goods-in-transit orders. You maintain a high level of product quality throughout the goods-in-transit process. You can monitor existing quality orders and take next steps directly from the goods-in-transit order form. This integration gives you better visibility and control over quality-related processes. It helps you take a proactive approach to maintaining and protecting product quality when you import goods from the Landed Cost module.

During the quality control process, you can block inventory from goods-in-transit orders to prevent the shipment of defective products. This function helps you follow regulatory compliance and reduce legal risks. The proactive approach of inventory blocking lowers rework costs by avoiding post-distribution corrections. By using this integration, you make sure that only high-quality products reach your customers. You build brand trust and loyalty.
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts

results.Feature name: Sync external changes through Inventory Visibility
results.Business value: When you integrate Dynamics 365 Supply Chain Management with external inventory systems, you usually need to synchronize inventory updates between systems, create order and journal records in Supply Chain Management, and aggregate data to optimize performance. Inventory Visibility provides built-in functionality that supports all these operations. This functionality reduces implementation efforts and improves system performance. With this solution, you can trace inventory transactions back to the original source documents and run historical inventory analyses based on the synchronized data.
results.Feature details:
The Inventory Visibility service for Supply Chain Management now enables external systems to synchronize, aggregate, and store cross-channel inventory transactions in the Supply Chain Management inventory adjustment journal. The system provides the following capabilities:

  • Post inventory changes with all the previously supported information. The post operation now includes extra information about the source document, such as the document type and order number, and the target inventory adjustment journal. The Inventory Visibility service provides new APIs to support these operations.
  • Automatically store transactions in Inventory Visibility based on your API input. Use these transactions for inventory analysis and other business purposes.
  • Set up inventory transaction aggregation rules in Inventory Visibility for synchronizing with Supply Chain Management.
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts

results.Feature name: Track and trace serial and batch numbers for manufacturing
results.Business value: Dynamics 365 Supply Chain Management provides an effective, accurate, and high-performance process for tracking serial and batch numbers of components and finished goods. This process helps manufacturers comply with industry standards and regulations. It also helps ensure the quality of processes such as maintenance, warranty, and recalls.
results.Feature details:
Dynamics 365 Supply Chain Management now enables manufacturers to:

  • Track serial and batch numbers for components used in production orders and link them to serial and batch numbers for finished goods.
  • Generate production orders with quantities greater than one unit, even when both components and finished products require serial or batch number control.
  • Set up the serial number and batch number tracking process to integrate seamlessly with job management.
  • Track serial and batch numbers from both the production floor execution interface and the standard UI for managing production orders.

To enable these features, Supply Chain Management connects to the supply chain traceability service. Supply Chain Management helps workers scan barcodes with serial and batch numbers and lets supervisors view and edit tracking details. The supply chain traceability service provides high-performance traceability and can connect to many other data sources as needed.
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts

results.Feature name: Enable quality control with acceptance sampling
results.Business value: Improve quality and reduce inspection time and costs by applying acceptance sampling, a statistical method based on industry standards, to determine optimal sample sizes and pass or fail criteria. This method enables efficient, risk-based quality control while minimizing manual effort.
results.Feature details:
This feature uses quality orders to perform acceptance sampling.

With acceptance sampling, workers inspect a statistically determined sample size of products to identify defects. Workers categorize each defect as critical, major, or minor. A code letter and a master sampling chart based on ISO and ANSI industry standards define the sample size and allowable defect counts for each category.

With this feature, you can:

  • Generate an acceptance sampling chart (image 1), which consists of a code letter chart (image 2) and an acceptable quality limit chart (image 3). The acceptable quality limit chart provides supporting data from a predefined template. Administrators can edit these templates.

  • Create a quality association that includes item sampling and test groups, with tests classified by defect type (critical, major, or minor). This configuration enables automatic generation of quality orders for acceptance sampling (image 4).

  • Print a test report that includes defect thresholds by category, test results, and a clear indication of whether the lot passes or fails inspection (image 5).
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts

results.Feature name: Achieve regulatory compliance and quality excellence
results.Business value: Integrate advanced quality management capabilities across the supply chain. This integration fosters continuous improvement, customer satisfaction, regulatory compliance, and quality excellence. Advanced quality management minimizes reliance on third-party quality management solutions. It ensures adherence to regulations, enhances product quality, and boosts customer satisfaction. This solution also fosters continuous improvement and risk mitigation through a feedback-driven strategy.
results.Feature details:
Advanced quality management integrates throughout the Supply Chain Management solution. Key features include:

  • Optimized testing strategies: Flexible sampling plans, skip-lot testing, quality order creation triggers, sample management, instrument calibration, and acceptable quality limits (AQL) testing.
  • Regulatory compliance: Approved customer lists, customer-specific certificates of analysis (COA), and enhancements to electronic signatures.
  • Digitized manufacturing for precision and compliance: Electronic batch records and production dispensing.
  • Continuous improvement and risk mitigation: Corrective and preventive actions (CAPA) management and enhancements to nonconformance.
  • Enhanced user experience: Simplified entry of quality test results and new workspaces for quality management.
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts

results.Feature name: Enable quality control with sample management
results.Business value: Sample management helps manufacturers keep product quality consistent, lower compliance risks, and make traceability better. With these benefits, manufacturers can efficiently meet regulatory standards and customer expectations.
results.Feature details:
With sample management, you can:

  • Define a plan for sampling finished goods that you produce with production or batch orders. Set how often to take samples, such as one sample from every 10th produced batch or license plate. You don't track samples in inventory. Instead, you define their lifecycle.

  • Define a plan for testing samples by using quality orders. Set how often to test the samples, such as testing every seventh sample.

  • Block the portion of produced goods that you didn't test. When a quality order passes, you automatically release the portion of produced goods.

  • Keep track of samples throughout their lifecycle, from creation to disposal.
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts

results.Feature name: Register time spent working on projects for production
results.Business value: This feature provides an effective way to track time spent on projects without requiring workers to fill out a timesheet every time they start and stop work. Instead, workers use the production floor execution interface to register their time directly. This capability is especially relevant when you track time for activities such as research and trial runs that aren't directly related to daily production work.
results.Feature details: Supervisors and managers set up the production floor execution interface so workers can register time for project activities. When a worker is ready to start a new project, they select the project from a list. The system records the start time. When the worker registers their next activity, such as clocking out or starting a new project or production job, the system registers the end time for the previous project. The system updates its journal with the project start and end times.
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts

results.Feature name: Decouple production registration from time and attendance
results.Business value: With this feature, workers can register production work in your manufacturing execution system (MES) without needing manager approval for workers' time registrations and adjustments. If your company doesn't use the time and attendance module in Dynamics 365 Supply Chain Management, this feature provides a faster workflow and simpler setup.
results.Feature details:
This feature separates the processes for manufacturing execution from the approval process for time and attendance.

Production managers configure Supply Chain Management to let managers skip the approval process for recorded work times when adjustments are applied, such as registration errors or unpaid time off. Now, production managers can end production orders without waiting for payroll managers to approve time and attendance.

With this feature, finance controllers can calculate production costs using estimated operation times instead of recorded production times. Use this option for production jobs where time estimates are usually more accurate than recorded times.
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts

results.Feature name: Build collaborative, accurate demand plans powered by AI
results.Business value: Demand planning for Dynamics 365 Supply Chain Management makes it easy to create highly accurate forecasts based on historical data. It uses powerful AI that you can configure in detail or with a simple click. You can override data with your own experience or insights from your team. Throughout the process, you can quickly collaborate with colleagues about specific parts of the forecast, and review versions of the forecasts with stakeholders before approving them. Data flows seamlessly between applications, so you get accurate end-to-end supply chain planning.
results.Feature details:
Planning is crucial for any business that wants to meet customer demand, optimize inventory, and reduce costs. However, planning can be challenging when you deal with multiple products, markets, and stakeholders. Planning is also challenging when you need to account for changing conditions and uncertainties. You need a planning solution that helps you create and work with demand plans that cover your entire organization. The solution supports multiple units of measure and currencies. It enables data-driven decisions. Demand planning for Dynamics 365 Supply Chain Management is such a solution. It offers a range of features and benefits that help you plan more effectively and efficiently, such as the following capabilities:

  • New product introduction and end of lifecycle management: Plan for the launch of new products and the phase-out of old products. Adjust your demand forecasts.

  • Row-level access: Control who can view and edit your plans. Assign different permissions to planners based on their roles and responsibilities.

  • Rolling plans: Continuously update your plans. Extend them to cover longer time horizons to keep up with changing demand and supply scenarios.

  • Excel integration and qualitative forecasting: Import and export your time series data directly to and from Excel.

  • Qualitative forecasting: Use qualitative methods, such as surveys and expert opinions, to supplement your quantitative forecasts.

  • Planner's workspace: Customize your dashboard with charts, key performance indicators, and alerts. Use it to monitor and manage your plans more easily. The planner's workspace is powered by Power BI, which provides advanced analytics and visualization capabilities.

  • Recalculation of related time series: Trigger the recalculation of your time series, either periodically or based on events, so your plans stay up to date and aligned with your data sources.

  • Filter on input data for calculations and forecasts: Apply filters to your input data, such as by product, location, or customer, to perform calculations and forecasts on specific segments or scenarios.

  • Scheduled jobs for data ingestion, transformation, calculation, and forecast: Automate the processes of importing, transforming, calculating, and forecasting your data. Schedule regular or on-demand forecast runs to save time and resources.

  • Time series decomposition: Break down your time series into components to better understand and forecast your demand patterns.

With these features and benefits, Microsoft Dynamics 365 Supply Chain Management helps you create accurate and collaborative plans that support your business goals and customer satisfaction.

The following images show examples of the Demand planning user interface for working with demand plans and setting up forecast calculations.
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts

results.Feature name: Use item substitution for bill of materials in master planning
results.Business value: Get the most value from your inventory by substituting components in a bill of materials based on availability.
results.Feature details:
Specify substitute components by priority for the main component on a bill of materials. The system uses these substitutes when the main component isn't available in inventory to fulfill the order.

When you run master planning, the system automatically uses the substitute component that's available in inventory. It follows the priority order in the plan group where you can specify which component to substitute.

This feature is available for Planning Optimization.
results.Enabled for: Admins, makers, marketers, or analysts, automatically

results.Feature name: Calculate capable-to-promise quantities in real time
results.Business value: Give customers realistic dates for when you can deliver specific goods. For each sales line, enter a date that accounts for existing on-hand inventory, production capacity, and transportation times.
results.Feature details:
Capable-to-promise (CTP) calculations in Dynamics 365 Supply Chain Management now consider both materials and resources when calculating delivery dates. This information is essential for assemble-to-order or make-to-order businesses. CTP calculations include all the components, purchases, transfers, and capacities needed to manufacture any specific item. You can run these calculations for any selected sales order line.

With accurate confirmed ship and receipt dates, you can:

  • Tell customers the exact dates when they receive their orders in real time, while you're on the phone.
  • Check how any change to a product, such as color, design, or other options, affects the promised delivery date.
  • Check how any change in quantity affects the promised delivery date.
  • Group or separate line quantities to meet delivery deadlines on make-to-order products.
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts

results.Feature name: Forecast with intelligence for validated demand plans
results.Business value: Inaccurate forecasts can lead to lost revenue and inefficiency in the supply chain. When you provide forecasts with intelligence, you create more accurate and effective demand plans. The direct impact shows up as improved revenue and fewer stockouts. Operating expenses go down in supply chains that need fewer fire drills.
results.Feature details:
This solution is Microsoft’s next-generation collaborative demand-planning solution for Dynamics 365 Supply Chain Management. It uses best-in-class forecasting algorithms and models. The solution offers immersive user experiences, intelligent reports, and analytics. It empowers organizations to build an agile, resilient, and sustainable demand-planning practice fueled by intelligence and collaboration. The solution offers:

  • A no-code approach to demand modeling and planning configuration. Flexible building blocks enable most demand planners (over 85%) who aren't data scientists to do what-if planning and analyze, optimize, and compare scenarios in minutes.

  • Seamless, on-the-fly aggregation and disaggregation, which enable planners to edit forecasts at a corporate or product-group level. Planners can then zoom in and instantly see the impact on the regional and stock keeping unit (SKU) level.

  • Improved forecast accuracy with automatic AI parameter tuning, which helps ensure accurate forecasting and preprocessing. External signals enable superior forecast accuracy by considering promotions or stockouts.

  • Disruption readiness with interactive and fast what-if analysis. Version history enables you to track and evaluate forecast changes. Use the lessons learned to improve the decision-making process.

  • Effective collaboration throughout the planning cycle by using Microsoft Teams in-context communication, in-product commenting, and restorable versions of forecast values throughout the planning process.

  • Increased agility through integrated planning and execution flow with native integration to Supply Chain Management, customizable worksheets, and exception-based planning.
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts

results.Feature name: Collaborate on supply plans within and across teams
results.Business value:
Master planning is a complex process that considers many factors when deciding which goods and materials to order to keep the business running smoothly. Multiple supply planners, production planners, material managers, and other stakeholders participate in the process. As a result, users working within and across teams often collaborate while entering relevant data and preparing and reviewing each plan.

To collaborate effectively, all contributors need access to the same up-to-date information. Dynamics 365 Supply Chain Management now provides greatly improved support for master planning collaboration because it continuously recalculates your master plans and refreshes them after each change. All planners and other stakeholders see the latest, fully optimized planning results as they evaluate and update each plan.
results.Feature details:
Master planning calculations use a lot of computer resources. To save resources, companies usually schedule these calculations to run overnight when the system isn't busy. This scheduling gives you a new set of optimized plans to review at the start of the next business day. Supply planners, production planners, and material managers can review and update each master plan throughout the day to handle new issues, like material delays, machine breakdowns, or changing priorities. As the day goes on, each modified plan becomes less optimized and harder to work with until the system recalculates the plans overnight.

Because the planning engine in Supply Chain Management works in parallel with the rest of the system, it can recalculate master plans during the day without affecting overall system performance. All collaborators always have access to freshly optimized supply plans. This approach ensures the best use of materials and capacity, and it minimizes delays when providing products to customers.
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts

results.Feature name: Enhance your demand forecasting with additional data inputs
results.Business value: The latest enhancements for demand planning in Dynamics 365 Supply Chain Management give you the ability to create forecasts that include both historical sales data and the effect of future events and promotions. Use these enhancements to help optimize your supply chain and improve your financial results.
results.Feature details:
Demand planning in Supply Chain Management helps you plan and forecast demand using various data sources and signals. The app now provides capabilities that enhance the accuracy of demand forecasts, so you can better optimize your supply chain and improve financial outcomes.

The new capabilities include:

  • Support for multiple signals: Define multiple input signals, such as inflation or weather-related measures, and apply the XGBoost forecast algorithm. This algorithm assesses correlation and analyzes the impact of each signal against historical sales data. It incorporates these insights into the forecast result.
  • Event and promotion planning: Define event and promotion types along with their related events and promotions. Each event type and its related events act as signals. The system assesses correlation and analyzes the impact of each signal against historical sales data. It incorporates these insights into the forecast result.
  • Additional forecast algorithms: Evaluate new forecast algorithms for specific scenarios.
results.Enabled for: Users, automatically

results.Feature name: Enhance your demand forecasting and planning
results.Business value: The latest enhancements for demand planning in Dynamics 365 Supply Chain Management provide new insights into your supply chain planning. Use these insights to help optimize your supply chain and improve your financial outcomes.
results.Feature details:
Demand planning in Supply Chain Management helps you plan and forecast demand based on various data sources and signals. The app now provides capabilities that enhance the accuracy of the demand forecast so you can better optimize your supply chain and boost your financial outcomes.

All the new capabilities are available by default. Use each capability as needed with your new and existing forecasts to help improve accuracy. The new capabilities include:

  • Time freeze: Set up periods when forecast calculations that run continuously don't update their baseline forecasts.
  • Forecast with signals: Forecast based on multiple input signals by using calculations based on the Prophet forecast algorithm, XGBoost forecast algorithm, or best-fit forecast model. The best-fit forecast model automatically calculates both XGBoost and Prophet results for each combination of dimensions and chooses the best one for the final forecast.
  • Auto detect seasonality: Automatically detect seasonality patterns for all forecast algorithms. The system runs an algorithm to detect distinct seasonality patterns across different dimensions, such as products and locations.
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts

results.Feature name: Enhance your demand forecasting and planning
results.Business value: The latest enhancements for demand planning in Microsoft Dynamics 365 Supply Chain Management give you new insights into your supply chain planning. Use these insights to optimize your supply chain and improve your financial results.
results.Feature details:
Demand planning in Microsoft Dynamics 365 Supply Chain Management helps you plan and forecast demand using various data sources and signals. This release enhances the app by adding the following capabilities:

  • Filter data on calculation profiles.
  • View and select the best model for each planning unit based on accuracy metrics.
  • Add external signals, such as inflation rates or weather conditions, to your forecast calculation and see how they affect your demand.
  • Use a new forecast model, XGBoost, which creates forecasts with both input and signal data.
  • Use time-fence rules to block edits during specific locked or closed periods.
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts

results.Feature name: Implement lean manufacturing, catch weight, and step consumption
results.Business value: The Planning Optimization planning engine helps businesses streamline their planning processes, lower costs, and improve service levels by creating fast and accurate supply plans. This planning engine makes the supply chain more agile and responsive. Planning Optimization now includes features that let companies using lean manufacturing, catch weight pricing and measurement systems, and step consumption with process manufacturing benefit from this planning engine.
results.Feature details:
Planning Optimization provides the features that support the following business processes:

  • Lean manufacturing: A production philosophy that maximizes value for customers while minimizing waste. It comes from the Toyota production system and focuses on continuous improvement and operational efficiency.

  • Catch weight: A pricing and measurement system used mainly in the food industry. Products are sold based on their actual weight instead of a predetermined fixed weight. This system is important for items that naturally vary in size and weight, such as meat, seafood, and fresh produce.

  • Step consumption: Manages material consumption to accommodate non-linear consumption rates that are based on production quantities.
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts

results.Feature name: Approve POs and requisitions from mobile device
results.Business value: Many businesses use approval workflows to ensure that all new purchase requisitions and purchase orders are valid, within budget, and aligned with business practices and standards. However, this extra layer of security can sometimes delay the purchasing process. To address this challenge, Dynamics 365 Supply Chain Management offers a mobile approval experience that brings flexibility, efficiency, resiliency, and responsiveness to the approval process.
results.Feature details:
Users who approve purchase requisitions and purchase orders can now respond to approval workflow tasks from their mobile devices. Procurement approval activities include approvals related to purchase requisitions, requisition lines, purchase orders, and order lines.

The mobile app shows an overview of the user's assigned approval tasks and due dates. From there, the user can open details for each purchase requisition and purchase order to approve, all without switching context. After reviewing the context and details of an approval request, the user selects the appropriate response option. The user chooses from the approval options configured for the workflow: approve, reject, request change, or delegate to another user.

For information about how to prepare your Supply Chain Management and Dataverse environments to support the Approvals Management mobile app and how to install the app on your mobile devices, see Onboard the Approvals Management mobile app.

The following screenshots show examples of how the app looks when running on a mobile device.
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts

results.Feature name: Integrate contract lifecycle management in source to pay
results.Business value: With flexible integration to external contract lifecycle management systems, businesses can efficiently manage the creation and management of contract lifecycles with suppliers in their source-to-pay process. Purchase agreements stipulated in the contracts integrate seamlessly with operations such as applying the correct terms and pricing.
results.Feature details: Contract lifecycle management (CLM) is a key element of the source-to-pay process. Dynamics 365 Supply Chain Management now provides a platform that makes it easy to integrate with an external CLM system. The feature supports out-of-the-box rules and integration points for interacting with CLM. The integration supports purchase agreements and non-disclosure agreements. It uses master data from Supply Chain Management.
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts

results.Feature name: Manage supplier relationships with Supplier Engagement
results.Business value: With the Supplier Engagement app, procurement professionals can manage supplier relationships for all legal entities from one workspace. Procurement professionals can view supplier performance, schedule appointments, and manage many other aspects of their supplier relationships. A new supplier portal gives suppliers a better experience when onboarding and engaging with procurement professionals. The portal supports collaboration throughout the relationship, including during requests for quotation, purchase ordering, and invoicing.
results.Feature details:
The new Supplier Engagement application gives you a Microsoft Power Apps experience that integrates with Dynamics 365 Supply Chain Management and Dynamics 365 Finance. With this app, you can manage both strategic and tactical areas of your supplier relationships. You can schedule appointments, track performance, schedule supplier reviews, and manage prospective suppliers who aren't registered yet.

The experience also includes a supplier-facing portal based on Microsoft Power Pages. The new supplier portal supports many facets of the supplier relationship, ranging from tactical to operational and transactional aspects. It helps new suppliers onboard and enables existing suppliers to engage with your company through transactions such as requests for quotation (RFQs), managed bidding, purchase orders, and invoices. The supplier portal works with the existing vendor collaboration capabilities in Supply Chain Management.
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts

results.Feature name: Manage compliance with export control restrictions
results.Business value: Companies that trade internationally and follow external regulations and internal policies face challenges when they keep track of the many different rules and policies. With the new advanced export control functionality, you can express these rules, including complex ones, by using formulas like those in Microsoft Excel. You can ensure that the system honors these rules throughout the sales and fulfillment process.
results.Feature details:
Dynamics 365 Supply Chain Management now lets you manage, track, and verify compliance with export control restrictions before you confirm, pack, ship, and invoice sales orders.

The system lets you manage export control policies by using a native Microsoft Dataverse solution that directly connects with your Supply Chain Management instance. Supply Chain Management enforces compliance with international trade regulations by checking your export-control policies in real time.

With the Dataverse-based architecture, many of the other systems you use can also access your export control rules through the hundreds of connectors available for Dataverse. You can also use the rich features of Microsoft Power Platform, like the Microsoft Power Fx language, to extend the solution. Your export control administrator can support complex scenarios by creating formulas like those in Microsoft Excel.
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts

results.Feature name: Inspect and process returned items more efficiently
results.Business value: Get more control and flexibility over the customer return process. Warehouse operators can inspect returned goods and choose to postpone the disposition code selection until the inspection is done.
results.Feature details:
This feature makes it easier for warehouse operators to manage and review returned items and choose how to process the return. Warehouse workers don't need to select a disposition code when they receive a returned item. Instead, they can choose to postpone that decision until after they inspect the item.

Warehouse workers can now create return orders by using the Warehouse Management mobile app Return item receiving menu item. This feature supports blind receiving and return details receiving processes.

The enhanced process for working with return labels provides the following capabilities:

  • Return labels are now available during the manual packing process.
  • A new batch job cleans up return data, return-label information, and expiration dates when the return validity period ends.

All businesses that deal with customer returns in the warehouse can gain greater control and flexibility over their return process while improving productivity and the user experience.
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts

results.Feature name: Improve operations with Warehouse Management mobile app V4
results.Business value: In version 4, the Warehouse Management mobile app moves from Xamarin to React Native. With this change, you get richer and more advanced mobile experiences, faster updates, and innovative features that evolve with your business needs.
results.Feature details:
Warehouse Management mobile app version 4 is a major upgrade that moves the app to a modern foundation. With this foundation, the app delivers multiple features, benefits, and improvements.

New features

This version introduces:

  • A variety of color themes and sound options for a personalized experience.
  • An advanced camera-based barcode scanner for faster, more accurate scanning.
  • Performance enhancements for smoother operation.
  • Improved network resilience to keep you connected in challenging conditions.
  • Built-in Wi-Fi self-diagnostics to help troubleshoot connectivity issues on the go.

Benefits

Version 4 improves the user experience and productivity of warehouse tasks.

Added value

Version 4 provides a custom UI experience for your warehouse workers. It delivers improved performance and adds Wi-Fi monitoring.

Impact

Warehouse workers benefit from the new user experience and get a working tool for optimized task processing.

Screenshots

The following screenshots show the Wi-Fi diagnostics feature, the ability to choose from several captured barcodes, and the full set of new dark and light color themes.
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts

results.Feature name: Integrate the Warehouse management module with external labor management systems
results.Business value: Now you can connect the Warehouse management module in Supply Chain Management to the external labor management system that you choose. This connection helps you plan labor more effectively, track performance, and reduce administrative overhead. With this connection, you get deeper insights into labor costs, improve employee engagement, and boost productivity.
results.Feature details:
This feature provides a standard framework for integrating the Warehouse management module in Supply Chain Management with external labor management systems (LMS). With this framework, you can connect warehouse execution data to a third-party LMS system. This connection gives you advanced capabilities for labor planning, scheduling, and performance tracking. The integration supports warehouse execution with or without warehouse management processes (WMS). It also supports warehouse-only mode.

Labor management systems typically provide the following features:

  • Real-time productivity monitoring and task tracking
  • Engineered labor standards and support for time studies such as MOST, MTM, and MSD
  • Predictive scheduling and dynamic labor forecasting
  • Customizable dashboards and ad-hoc reporting
  • Incentive program management, alerts, and supervisor workflows
  • Mobile access and role-based views for managers and workers
  • Automated time and attendance tracking
  • Visibility into cost-to-serve and opportunity costs

With this integration, you can streamline communication between warehouse operations and workforce management. You can also reduce manual data entry and improve compliance and auditability.
results.Enabled for: Users, automatically

results.Feature name: Operate external shared warehouse with Warehouse management only mode
results.Business value: Supply Chain Management enables organizations to consolidate logistics operations into one legal entity that manages warehousing, inventory, and transportation for multiple legal entities. For example, you might have sales offices in several states or countries that share a single central department for warehousing, inventory, and deliveries. This setup makes large organizations more efficient by letting each legal entity focus on the business areas where it excels.
results.Feature details:
Warehouse management-only mode lets you create a legal entity in Dynamics 365 Supply Chain Management that manages warehousing and logistics operations for other Supply Chain Management legal entities. This functionality builds on the existing ability of warehouse management-only mode to manage warehousing operations for external ERP systems.

Warehouse management-only mode processes purchase and sales orders with new, lightweight source documents for inbound and outbound shipment orders. These documents focus on warehouse management and can replace multiple types of documents that legal entities previously exchanged, such as sales orders, purchase orders, and transfer orders.

The system uses a new owner inventory dimension to track ownership of catalog items that multiple legal entities share.

This functionality extends the capabilities of the Operate warehouses connected to external order management systems feature that we announced in late 2023. With this functionality, your logistics operations can manage both internal and external warehouse processes in the same warehouse management system and physical locations.
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts

results.Feature name: Capture worker IDs for warehouse packing events
results.Business value: The Warehouse Management mobile app now captures worker IDs during key container handling steps, so the system can trace packing activities. This traceability feature ensures accountability, improves operational transparency, and supports audit readiness. It helps you identify training needs or process gaps by providing clear visibility into who performs each packing task and when. With this insight, you can enhance overall warehouse efficiency and accuracy.
results.Feature details:
The Warehouse Management mobile app now captures worker IDs during key container handling steps, such as when a worker adds items to a container or closes the container. With this update, Dynamics 365 Supply Chain Management can trace all packing activities.

Benefits

Provides full traceability of who performs each packing action and when.

Added value

Enhances accountability and transparency in warehouse operations. By logging worker IDs at each step, the system supports quality control, reduces errors, and enables quick issue resolution when discrepancies arise.

Impact

This feature improves operational efficiency and audit readiness. Managers get visibility into packing workflows, so they can track performance, find training opportunities, and keep up with internal or external standards.
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts

results.Feature name: Improve picking efficiency with wrist-mounted scanning devices
results.Business value: Wrist-mounted scanners let warehouse workers scan barcodes without juggling a separate device. This feature eliminates repetitive motions and can save three to five seconds per scan. With real-time feedback and an ergonomic design, wrist-mounted scanners help reduce picking and packing errors. Some organizations see up to a 27% improvement in picking accuracy. The lightweight, wearable design reduces physical strain and fatigue. This reduction leads to higher worker satisfaction and lower injury risk. The value of this design is especially high in environments with high turnover or labor shortages.
results.Feature details:
The Warehouse Management mobile app for Dynamics 365 Supply Chain Management now runs natively on many wrist-mounted scanning devices, such as those sold by ProGlove.

  • Improve accuracy: Reduce picking and packing errors. Some organizations see up to a 27% improvement in picking accuracy when they switch to wrist-mounted scanners.
  • Improve productivity: Save three to five seconds per scan. In high-volume environments, this time savings adds up to several hours each day.
  • Reduce physical strain and fatigue: Improve worker satisfaction and reduce injury risk.
  • Rapid deployment: Quickly roll out the mobile app to many wrist-mounted scanning devices.

The following images show examples of wrist-mounted scanners in use.
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts

results.Feature name: Optimize processes with agent-led time, expense, and approvals
results.Business value: Copilot and agentic automation detect work from Outlook and Teams. By streamlining time entry, expense report creation, and approval processes, the solution reduces administrative burdens and improves invoice accuracy. This business value results in faster invoicing, fewer errors, and greater satisfaction for project managers, consultants, and invoice administrators.
results.Feature details:
By using Copilot and agentic automation, you can remove inefficiencies in the invoice process. The solution automatically detects work from Microsoft Outlook calendars and Microsoft Teams meetings. These features enable automated creation and submission of time entries. You don't need alerts and reminders that often frustrate project managers, consultants, and invoice administrators.

The automation also helps create, submit, and check policy for expense reports from emails and receipt scans. It includes automatic policy validations. Project managers can upload documents with rules to flag approvals that need review while allowing others to approve automatically.

Overall, these advanced technologies use natural language inferences and automation to help project-centered companies submit accurate invoices, reduce corrections, and remove frustrations and last-minute issues for everyone involved.
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts
results.GeographicAreasDetails:
We're releasing this feature into the following Microsoft Azure geographic areas:

  • Norway
  • South Africa
  • Switzerland
  • United Arab Emirates
  • United States
  • Europe
  • Asia Pacific
  • United Kingdom
  • Australia
  • Brazil
  • Canada
  • India
  • Japan
  • France

results.Feature name: Use Copilot for time entry
results.Business value: Introducing Copilot for time entry, powered by generative AI that simplifies how you log time.
results.Feature details:
Copilot acts as your intelligent assistant and takes away some of the heavy lifting associated with logging work completed by you, all while ensuring high accuracy and compliance.

Team members can ask Copilot for time entry to create their time entries for them, without having to navigate through numerous forms or fill in details using dropdown fields. Copilot for time entry can use details shared by the team member or existing project assignments to create multiple time entries together, while keeping existing validations in check. In addition to this, Copilot for time entry can also generate a first draft for external comments across time entries. These comments can then be modified via the copilot sidecar without the use of individual forms for every time entry.

Users may be asked to confirm the details, if required, before new entries are created to ensure accurate tracking of their progress.
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts
results.GeographicAreasDetails:
This feature will be released into the following Microsoft Azure geographic areas:

  • Switzerland
  • United Arab Emirates
  • United States
  • Europe
  • Asia Pacific
  • United Kingdom
  • Australia
  • Brazil
  • Canada
  • India
  • Japan
  • France

results.Feature name: Match credit card transactions and itemize expenses automatically
results.Business value: The Expense Agent brings significant operational and user experience improvements to the expense management process by automatically matching credit card transactions and itemizing expenses. This feature intelligently analyzes incoming credit card transactions, matches them with relevant receipts based on transaction details, and breaks them down into itemized entries as needed. By using the Expense Agent, you reduce your workload, minimize manual errors, and ensure seamless expense management.
results.Feature details:
You can enable both auto credit card matching and itemization features. Choose the features that fit your organization's needs by setting specific parameters in the finance and operations environment.

When you enable this feature, the Expense Agent automatically matches credit card transactions with corresponding receipts and intelligently itemizes expenses. This feature removes the need for manual entry. When the agent detects a credit card transaction, it analyzes the transaction data—such as amount, date, and merchant details. It compares this data with the various uploaded receipts to find the best match.

In addition to matching, the agent itemizes complex expenses, such as hotel bills or group meals. It breaks down the total amount into line items based on receipt data or predefined rules. For example, the agent auto-itemizes a hotel invoice with room charges, taxes, and meals into distinct categories. Each category is accurately tagged.

This functionality significantly reduces the time you spend processing expenses, ensures data accuracy, and maintains compliance with organizational policies. It also enables finance teams to receive more detailed and structured expense data, so they can improve reporting and reconciliation processes.
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts

results.Feature name: Use Copilot enhancements to tailor project status reports
results.Business value: With this enhancement, project managers get the flexibility to tailor report sections to fit their specific business needs and address diverse audiences. This feature makes sure reports meet the unique requirements of each project.
results.Feature details: This feature uses the insight generator to capture data from predefined entities. Users can add these entities to the project status report. Users can also save and share the report with different audiences.
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts
results.GeographicAreasDetails:
This feature will be available in the following Microsoft Azure geographic areas:

  • Switzerland
  • United Arab Emirates
  • United States
  • Europe
  • Asia Pacific
  • United Kingdom
  • Australia
  • Brazil
  • Canada
  • India
  • Japan
  • France

results.Feature name: Add custom columns to an enterprise task, phase 1
results.Feature details:
Enterprise task custom columns (ETCC) is a feature that lets you add columns from the project tasks table to the grid view in Project for the web. First, you define your new column in Microsoft Dataverse on the Project tasks table. The supported types of columns in this phase of the feature are text, date, and number. When you add the column to the Project tasks table, a new menu appears in the Project for the web iframe. You use this menu to add the column to the grid view. Project for the web pulls data from Dataverse and displays it.

This phase of ETCC supports a read-only view inside Project for the web. To update the custom column, use the project tasks in Project Operations or make updates directly in Dataverse. When a user reloads the Project for the web iframe, changes in data refresh. Future phases of this feature support more column types and the ability to update data directly from Project for the web.
results.Enabled for: Users, automatically

results.Feature name: Expense app offers intuitive reimbursement experience
results.Feature details: The new expense mobile app for Project Operations enables you to enter expense details while you're on the go. We designed this feature to be flexible and adaptable. After the general availability release, we plan to add customer-requested feedback and enhancements.
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts

results.Feature name: Use stocked products in resource-based deployments
results.Feature details: The experiences for using inventoried products in Project Operations use the Inventory and Warehouse modules of Dynamics 365 Supply Chain Management. To accurately estimate the availability of stocked items and their costs, the system uses Pricing and Inventory visibility services. Use the Inventory close process in Supply Chain Management to update the project cost and show accurate consumption of project budgets. Providing all the capabilities needed to manage stocked items on projects takes multiple release waves.
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts

results.Feature name: Use the new Calendar interface for time entry
results.Business value: <!--bv-->Every organization needs an efficient time entry experience for its consultants and team members. The experience gives users the flexibility to log time in different ways. It minimizes the number of clicks and effort required to enter time.
results.Feature details:
The web experience for time entry in Dynamics 365 Project Operations is getting a series of improvements that make time entry faster and more accurate. The improvements give team members better tracking capabilities.

These upgrades include:

  • A new calendar interface to view, create, and change time entries.
  • The ability to track time across different timelines, such as weekly or monthly.
  • Tighter integration of the work breakdown structure for assigned tasks and projects within the time entry creation step (quick create forms).
  • Improved tracking with benchmarking of actual versus target hours and visualizations that help team members track their progress on project tasks.
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts

results.Feature name: Customize the Billing hub view
results.Business value: Billing hub enables project managers to create proforma invoices in Project Operations, so they can increase invoicing efficiency and get faster revenue recognition. When you customize existing forms and views on Billing hub, you can personalize the billing and invoice creation experience to suit your business needs.
results.Feature details:
With this feature, you can customize the Billing hub at all levels of the nested grid, including contract, contract line, and contract line views. The feature is automatically enabled for admins so you can customize the Billing hub forms and views to fit your organization's needs. For example, with this update, you can add more columns like Invoice ID to the contract view or add Discount % and Fee % to your contract line view in the Billing hub.

This release includes the following fixes:

  • Platform bug fix for the grid customizer that makes contract level customization possible.
  • Ability to customize views on contract lines for time, material, expense, fee, and milestones.
results.Enabled for: Admins, makers, marketers, or analysts, automatically

results.Feature name: Utilize repaired precision feature with certain values
results.Business value: By adjusting precision for values that come from Project for the web, you fix precision issues for assignments, bookings, resource requirements, and time entry values.
results.Feature details: Microsoft Project for the web and Microsoft Dynamics 365 Project Operations use different levels of precision for certain values. This difference can cause incorrect values for assigned effort. For example, you might see 79.5 hours when you expect 80 hours. These incorrect values can cause issues with bookings, time entry, and accounting. This feature fixes the difference in precision so that you see and use accurate values.
results.Enabled for: Users, automatically

results.Feature name: Experience the phase 3 enhancements for the Expense mobile app
results.Business value: By enhancing the Expense Management mobile app, you improve its efficiency and intuitiveness. You address common usability issues that customers raise through direct feedback. Category icons provide quicker visual recognition and selection, so users spend less time on data entry. When users rename receipts directly in the app, they enhance organization and make it easier to manage attachments on the go. These updates streamline your workflow, reduce friction in mobile expense reporting, and create a more seamless and productive user experience.
results.Feature details:
As part of our ongoing investment in improving the mobile experience, we introduced enhancements to the Expense Management mobile app that are based on direct customer feedback. These enhancements improve usability and streamline the overall user experience. This release includes the following improvements:

  • Add icons for each category: This enhancement adds icons for each expense category and displays them alongside category names in the mobile app. With these icons, you can quickly recognize and select the right category.

  • Ability to rename receipts in the mobile app: This enhancement enables you to rename receipts directly within the mobile app. With this feature, you can easily identify receipts without opening each attachment.
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts

results.Feature name: Enable contract line based default financial dimensions for expenses
results.Business value: By integrating financial dimension defaulting rules for expense transactions and reports, you streamline expense management, enhance data consistency, and ensure better compliance with financial policies.
results.Feature details:
This capability builds on the Enable flexibility in determining financial dimension defaulting for resource-based/non-stocked scenarios feature.

With this capability, organizations can apply dimension defaulting configuration rules to automatically get financial dimensions from contract lines or the related project for expense lines. This enhancement gives you more flexibility and control over how you apply default financial dimensions to expense-related documents. It improves data accuracy and reduces manual entry.
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts

results.Feature name: Default cost rate in project estimation scenarios
results.Business value: Currently, in Dynamics 365 Project Operations, users see the markup cost only in actuals, based on the markup price configuration on a price list. The system doesn't show the markup cost in project estimates, quote line details (QLD), contract line details (CLD), or project budgets. This situation causes confusion because users see different prices in actuals when compared to estimates, QLD, CLD, and budgets.
results.Feature details:
In the current implementation of Dynamics 365 Project Operations, the system limits visibility of the markup cost to the markup price configuration on a price list within the actuals.

Users see the markup cost in the actuals, but they don't see it in other critical areas such as project estimates, QLD, CLD, and project budgets.

This limitation causes confusion and inconsistency because the prices in the actuals differ from those in the estimates, QLD, CLD, and budgets.

This discrepancy causes misunderstandings and potential errors in project planning and financial tracking. The lack of visibility into the markup cost across all relevant sections creates a fragmented view of the project's financials.

Users can't accurately compare and reconcile the costs. This limitation creates challenges in maintaining a cohesive and transparent financial overview of projects.

With this release, the cost rate defaulting in project estimation scenarios respects and shows the markup cost across all estimates, QLD, CLD, and budgets.
results.Enabled for: Users, automatically

results.Feature name: New user experience for copy project
results.Business value: Currently, users can copy a project by selecting the Copy Project option on the main project form, which uses the Copy Project V3 API. However, this API doesn't support copying team members and their mappings. The Copy Project V4 API supports copying team members and their mappings, but it doesn't have a user interface.
results.Feature details:
This feature combines the capabilities of both versions 3 and 4 into a single, user-friendly interface. Users can copy projects to fit their needs.

On the user interface, users select:

  • Source project
  • Target project
  • Team member mapping options:
    • Don't copy team members
    • Replace team members with generic team members
    • Copy the named or generic team members in the source project
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts

results.Feature name: Implement access restrictions in Project for the web iFrame
results.Business value: Currently, when a user accesses a project record, they have unlimited access to the Microsoft Project for the web iFrame. There, they can modify, create, or delete any project tasks, dependencies, goals, and more. This feature adds the ability to manage what users can do in this iFrame.
results.Feature details: This feature adds a security system that lets administrators restrict what users can access inside the Project for the web iFrame on projects they access but don't join as team members. Project managers can use the same system to set restrictions for team members on their projects.
results.Enabled for: Users, automatically

results.Feature name: Increase limit to 1,500 tasks per project
results.Business value: Customers running large, complex projects across various industry verticals often require detailed project plans to sequence and assign their work with a level of granularity that mitigates project risks and drives clarity in each work package. While task checklists provide a way for program managers to add extra detail, they don't affect the schedule and aren't always a suitable alternative.
results.Feature details: This feature gives you an enhanced capability to generate comprehensive work breakdown structures. You can create structures that are 50% larger than the current limit, so you can include a much greater number of project tasks. This feature adds flexibility and complexity to project planning and management.
results.Enabled for: Users, automatically

results.Feature name: Use fixed exchange rate agreements for project contracts
results.Business value: Customers who use Project Operations for resource or nonstock deployments can currently use only fluctuating exchange rates. With this release, you can enter a fixed exchange rate to meet contractual or regulatory requirements.
results.Feature details: This feature adds the ability to configure a fixed exchange rate for a project contract. When you set the fixed exchange rate, all project transactions use the exchange rate to convert from the contract currency to the accounting currency for the legal entity.
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts

results.Feature name: Enable selective import from staging for Project Invoices
results.Business value: Dynamics 365 Project Operations manages large volumes of customer invoices that scheduled batch jobs usually process. However, users sometimes need to generate and process an invoice right away, such as when they correct an error or respond to an urgent request. With this feature, users can import and process individual invoice proposals without waiting for the full batch process. This feature makes project billing workflows more agile and responsive.
results.Feature details:
With this feature, project managers can quickly process a single invoice proposal directly from the open invoice proposal.

Previously, users waited for the scheduled import from the staging table or manually triggered the process that imported all invoice proposals. With this update, users can selectively import only the invoice lines for a specific proposal.

Key capabilities include the following features:

  • A new button to populate invoice lines in the context of a specific invoice proposal.
  • A new button on the Invoice Proposals list page to populate lines for a selected invoice.
  • Streamlined logic that limits data processing to the selected invoice proposal, so you get faster performance and reduced processing overhead.
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts

results.Feature name: Increase work breakdown structure limits
results.Business value: Enhance customer satisfaction by improving the performance of the work breakdown structure.
results.Feature details:
We implement this feature in phases. In phase 1, we increase the limits for:

  • Number of tasks in a project.
  • Number of assignments in a task.
  • Number of assignments in a project.

In phase 2, we enhance overall performance after we increase these limits. This enhancement reduces performance-related tickets and boosts customer satisfaction with Project Operations.
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts

results.Feature name: Experience the phase 2 enhancements for the Expense Mobile app
results.Business value: Enhance the Expense Management mobile app to improve usability, policy compliance, and workflow consistency across platforms. When you align mobile functionality with the finance and operations environment, you get a more intuitive and policy-aware experience that helps reduce errors, improve data quality, and enable faster decision-making. The redesigned workflow, enhanced validations, and better visibility for approvers help streamline expense reporting on the go, increase employee productivity, and strengthen organizational control over expense policies.
results.Feature details:
As part of our investment in improving the mobile experience, we're introducing enhancements to the Expense Management mobile app. These enhancements come from user feedback. These updates bring feature parity with the finance and operations environment. They address usability gaps and align with policy configurations. This release includes the following improvements:

  1. Change the design to create an Expense Report and then add expense line items: The current workflow lets you directly add expense line items. This enhancement restructures the workflow to require you to create an expense report first. After you create the report, you can add expense line items.

  2. Enable expense report header justification in the mobile app: This enhancement enables mobile app users to enter a justification based on the configured category and policy rules.

  3. Prevent selection of a parent activity when Block Parent Activity Selection is set to Do not allow: The mobile app currently lets you select a parent activity even when you set the Block Parent Activity Selection parameter to Do not allow in the finance and operations environment. With this enhancement, the mobile app respects the configuration you set in finance and operations apps.
