results.Feature name: Analyze quality of responses that use generative AI
results.Business value: Get insights into the quality of your knowledge sources and how well the agent responds to different types of questions. With this information, you can better serve your agent users.
results.Feature details:
Quality of response analyzes a sample set of successfully answered generative AI questions to provide insights about the quality of generative AI responses.

The feature uses AI to check the relevance, completeness, and grounding of each response in the sample set. It assigns a quality score for each response. The feature uses the quality score to classify each response as having good or poor quality.

Makers see a visual indicator that shows the breakdown of answered generative AI questions between good and poor quality. Makers can view the reasons for poor quality responses. They can see if the response isn't fully relevant, if the response is incomplete, or if the response isn't fully grounded.
results.Enabled for: Admins, makers, marketers, or analysts, automatically

results.Feature name: Get responses based on uploaded images
results.Business value: Image analysis expands the use cases for your custom copilots and delivers more informed responses. With image analysis, users get richer interactions and more empowering experiences.
results.Feature details:
They say a picture is worth 1,000 words. With image analysis, Copilot users can convey complex scenarios, objects, or ideas without typing lengthy descriptions. Copilot gets context from visual cues, which broadens its conversational range and yields more informed responses.

For example, when a user uploads an image of a car and asks, "What model is this car?", Copilot analyzes the image and responds with the car model. Later in the conversation, if a user asks, "What is the range of this car?", Copilot uses the previous image as context to answer the question.

Whether it's explaining a recipe from a food photo, getting advice on troubleshooting a broken printer, or identifying a rare bird species, Copilot provides detailed insights through image analysis.
results.Enabled for: Admins, makers, marketers, or analysts, automatically
results.GeographicAreasDetails:
We release this feature into the following Microsoft Azure geographic area:

  • United States

results.Feature name: Get analytics for autonomous agent runs
results.Business value: Improve the effectiveness of your autonomous agent with detailed analytics on usage and performance of your autonomous agent runs. Get visibility into how the agent performs and insights on how to improve it.
results.Feature details:
Analytics for autonomous agents gives you a comprehensive view of how autonomous runs perform. It includes:

  • Total number of agent runs.
  • Breakdown of runs by triggers.
  • Overall success rate for agent runs.
  • Success rate for autonomous agent runs over time.
  • Tools analysis so you can see tools usage and success rate.
  • Details on how long autonomous agent runs take, with a breakdown of successful and failed runs.
  • Billing consumption analysis for autonomous agents.
results.Enabled for: Admins, makers, marketers, or analysts, automatically

results.Feature name: Get smart actionable insights to improve your copilot agent performance
results.Business value: Improve the effectiveness of your copilot by using enhanced insights to understand how your copilot helps your users. Get AI-based insights that guide you on improvements so you can maximize your business goals.
results.Feature details:
Copilot admins and makers can get actionable insights directly on the agent's Analytics page:

  • See insights for unanswered Generative AI questions.
  • Get insights that focus on specific themes of questions.
  • Receive guidance on how to fix the issue and boost performance.
  • View the list of related conversations with unanswered questions so you can easily investigate the issue.

Insights are generated daily by checking all Gen AI unanswered questions from the past seven days.
This feature doesn't support SharePoint and Graph Connector knowledge sources.
results.Enabled for: Admins, makers, marketers, or analysts, automatically

results.Feature name: Configure enterprise-level security for Copilot Studio
results.Business value: Improvements in authentication, default permissions, governance settings, and auditing logs give makers more visibility and control with less risk when they create and deploy custom copilots.
results.Feature details:
The solution applies safer defaults and gives opinionated in-product communication when makers change those defaults. For example, the solution changes two significant security defaults. All bots require authenticated access, and makers don't have access to transcripts by default.

The solution also introduces these improvements:

  • Granular governance levers that give admins more control over the copilots in their tenants without affecting maker adoption.

    • Customers commonly request this feature. The solution invests in granular controls for many Copilot Studio features, including generative AI, knowledge sources, app insights, and authentication.

  • Audit log and inventory capabilities for admins.

    • Admins can see comprehensive audit logs, including viewing tenant-wide usage, inventory (including API support), and tenant hygiene (DLP violations and inactive copilots) reports to monitor business impact.

  • Show sensitive labels to the copilot creators and users when the response includes generative answers from SharePoint files.
results.Enabled for: Admins, makers, marketers, or analysts, automatically

results.Feature name: Automate approvals decisions with Intelligent Approvals
results.Business value: Intelligent approvals transform how organizations manage routine approvals by reducing turnaround times from days or even weeks to just minutes. This acceleration helps businesses stay on schedule, reduce delays, capture early payment discounts, and avoid costly late fees. By automating repetitive decisions, teams reclaim valuable time to focus on strategic, high-impact work. The feature also enhances accuracy in approvals, minimizing costly errors and financial risk, while delivering clear, audit-ready rationales that streamline compliance and regulatory reviews.
results.Feature details: Intelligent approvals is an AI-powered stage in advanced approvals action flows. With this capability, makers don't need to route every approval to a human. Instead, they configure an AI stage where a large language model evaluates the request using natural language prompts and contextual variables, such as text, documents, and images. The AI uses the defined logic to decide whether to approve or reject the request. Makers keep full control and decide if approvals are automatic or if humans can veto them.
results.Enabled for: Users, automatically

results.Feature name: Azure AI Search index as knowledge source
results.Business value: Makers can build copilots that use prebuilt Azure AI Search indexes as knowledge sources for more relevant and accurate grounded responses.
results.Feature details: Makers can use prebuilt indexes (indexes that pro developers built) with minimal friction and low cost. Use these indexes as grounding data for your copilots that you create with Microsoft Copilot Studio. With these copilots, users can ask questions in natural language about their enterprise systems that they use within their Power Platform tenants. Microsoft builds on the natural language-to-query approach that it released with Microsoft Dataverse knowledge and extends the same approach to copilots that use Azure AI Search index as knowledge.
results.Enabled for: Admins, makers, marketers, or analysts, automatically

results.Feature name: Configure SharePoint as a knowledge source for agents
results.Business value: Makers can now use extra data sources, including SharePoint, when they build autonomous agents. This enhancement supports more use cases by giving agents access to a wider range of valuable information. With this information, agents give more accurate and contextually relevant responses. This improvement makes efficiency and effectiveness better.
results.Feature details:
In Copilot Studio, knowledge sources work with generative answers to enhance your agents' capabilities. When you integrate knowledge sources, your agents can use enterprise data from Power Platform, Dynamics 365, websites, and external systems to give your customers relevant information and insights.

This release introduces support for adding SharePoint as a knowledge source for makers building autonomous agents. Only authenticated users can access SharePoint, so information sharing is secure and reliable. With this release, makers can access SharePoint knowledge using their credentials. Soon, you'll be able to access SharePoint knowledge with end-user credentials.

To add SharePoint as a knowledge source, use the following steps:

  1. Open the agent.
  2. Go to the Overview or Knowledge pages, and select Add Knowledge.
  3. Select SharePoint from the list of options.
  4. Enter the SharePoint URL.
  5. Confirm your selection.
  6. Select Add to finish adding SharePoint as a knowledge source.
results.Enabled for: Admins, makers, marketers, or analysts, automatically

results.Feature name: Create Microsoft Copilot extensions with Copilot Studio
results.Business value: Organizations often struggle with the "many bots" problem. They can't find the right solution that balances discovery, routing, permissions, and capability for the unique scenarios that modern workers need. Copilot extensions solve this problem. They make it fast and easy for anyone in your organization to create useful, tailored Microsoft Copilot experiences that generate answers, take action, and provide information specific to your business, data, and employees.
results.Feature details:
Copilot extensions in Microsoft Copilot for Microsoft 365 are created by employees in your organization to retrieve information or perform actions through Copilot.

With this feature, you can create and share these extensions in a simple canvas that all Microsoft Copilot users can access. Employees in your organization can quickly and easily create work-specific extensions in a familiar experience. They don't need to learn a new app or service. The extensions are instantly available in Microsoft Teams or at https://microsoft365.com/chat.

Microsoft 365 Copilot is a seat-licensed product that includes the Microsoft Copilot Studio license, so anyone can be a maker. These new makers can use Copilot Extensions in Microsoft 365 Copilot. They can also browse the Microsoft 365 Copilot Extension store to install an extension. Makers see the create a copilot extension call to action and go to the studio.

For example, an employee might want to create an extension for Copilot to handle internal queries and requests for creating, managing, and approving tasks within an expense reporting system.

When the maker creates their copilot extension, they can expand its knowledge with data sources from SharePoint and Microsoft Graph. This expansion enables richer, more informed answers that are contextually scoped. Makers can also create actions. These actions connect the extension to enterprise and internet systems to perform tasks, like getting the weather or resolving a ticket in a customer management portal.

When you define the knowledge and actions for a copilot extension, publish the extension to Microsoft Copilot. End users can then get information from the knowledge sources and interact with the capabilities the actions offer, all within Microsoft Copilot.

Admins can trust these copilot extensions. All extensions use the same Microsoft large language model. Power Platform admins govern which Power Platform tools are available in a copilot action. Microsoft 365 admins control who can use the copilot extension.

results.Feature name: Build custom copilots easily with an updated look and feel
results.Business value: Reduce the time it takes to create a custom copilot or build extensions for Microsoft Copilot by accessing features in a navigational hierarchy that feels natural and familiar.
results.Feature details:
This new user experience organizes the features and capabilities available to makers in Microsoft Copilot Studio.

A refreshed navigation menu and tab-based structure makes it easier to understand your place in the navigational hierarchy and what you can access:

  • The Home screen has a Describe with Copilot section that simplifies creating a custom copilot. It also offers custom copilot templates to help you get started.
  • The Library page shows your most recent copilots and extensions, so you can pick up where you left off. It also displays all your environment-level components.
  • The Configure page gives you an at-a-glance view of what a selected copilot can do.
  • The top menu bar provides access to knowledge, topics, actions, analytics, and channels, so you can see detailed views for each component.
  • Test your copilot anytime with the refreshed test pane.

results.Feature name: Add Confluence unstructured Spaces and Pages content as a knowledge source
results.Business value: Save time and increase productivity by using natural language queries in agents to access spaces and pages data that previously required navigating to Confluence client sources. Now you can integrate all knowledge data in an agent with other data sources to provide a single experience for information across multiple sources.
results.Feature details:
With this capability, makers can use Confluence Spaces and Pages content in unstructured storage as a knowledge source for their agents in Copilot Studio.

This capability enables agents to provide high-quality answers based on operational data within your Confluence configuration.

Administrators can manage access to this data by configuring the Confluence connector in the Administration Portal.

Makers can connect to Confluence as a knowledge source and select unstructured Spaces and Pages content that the agent uses when generating results or answering queries. You configure connections to knowledge sources within Copilot Studio when you create a new agent or configure or update an existing agent.

Dataverse processes and indexes content so it can give accurate, detailed results to natural language queries from agent users. Users get answers only from data they can access. The system checks user access tokens against Confluence content before it returns a response. Users can access the original articles through citation references.

Scheduled sync keeps the knowledge base data up to date, within a 4-6 hour window.
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts

results.Feature name: Add OneDrive files and folders as a knowledge source
results.Business value: Save time and increase productivity when makers add content from specific OneDrive files to Microsoft Copilot Studio agents. Users can access these files and folders along with any other knowledge sources they add to an agent. This capability removes the need to search through multiple files and folders outside of their existing agent-based operations.
results.Feature details:
With this capability, makers can use OneDrive file and folder content as a knowledge source for their agents in Copilot Studio.

This capability enables agents to provide high-quality answers based on operational data stored in files on OneDrive.

Administrators can manage access to this data by configuring the OneDrive connector in the Administration Portal.

Makers can connect to OneDrive as a knowledge source and select specific files or folders of files that the agent uses when generating results or answering queries. You configure connections to knowledge sources in Copilot Studio when you create a new agent or when you configure or update an existing agent.

Dataverse processes and indexes content so it can provide accurate, detailed results to natural language queries from agent users. Users see only answers based on data they can access. Dataverse validates user access tokens against OneDrive content before it returns a response. Users can access the original articles through citation references.

Scheduled sync keeps the knowledge base data up to date within a four- to six-hour window.

Administrators manage availability of this data by managing the OneDrive connector in the Administration Portal.

results.Feature details:

results.Feature name: Use the Lead Manager and Customer Brief templates
results.Business value: The Lead Manager and Customer Brief agent templates help you speed up customer adoption of autonomous agents by providing ready-made agents that connect to your existing line-of-business apps.
results.Feature details:
Two new autonomous agent templates show how to build an agent with a trigger that handles tasks on its own.

The new agent templates, the Lead Manager and Customer Brief, help customers start using autonomous agents faster.

The Lead Manager template focuses on sales development by managing incoming leads. It offers features like duplicate handling and automated email responses. It connects to Salesforce and Outlook. You can customize it by setting employee count thresholds for meeting scheduling.

The Customer Brief template makes meeting preparation easier by creating executive summaries from Salesforce data and automating email workflows.

You install both agents through Copilot Studio Managed Agents. You can edit the templates before publishing them and customize the templates to fit your business processes.
results.Enabled for: Admins, makers, marketers, or analysts, automatically

results.Feature name: Understand your agent's activity
results.Business value: To help makers during agent creation and once the agent is in production, both for agents that automate tasks or for conversational agents that interact with end users, makers get more details on the agent's activity at runtime to help troubleshoot issues and guide behavior.
results.Feature details:
In the Activity tab on your agent, you can review your agent's sessions. You can see transcripts of your user's interactions with your conversational agents when users choose to share them. This information helps you understand how the agent behaves in real-world scenarios so you can effectively debug and improve the agent. Agents that use generative orchestration have this functionality by default.

In the Activity list, you can see more information, including step count and last action, to help you identify the session you want.

When you view a specific agent run, you see what triggered the run, which actions the agent took, and the inputs and outputs for each action. You also see details about which knowledge sources the agent used and a detailed thought process to help with troubleshooting and understanding agent behavior.
results.Enabled for: Admins, makers, marketers, or analysts, automatically

results.Feature name: Solution management in Microsoft Copilot Studio
results.Business value: Unlike other products where customers rely on application lifecycle management (ALM) tools outside the product and from the market, solution management in Copilot Studio ensures ALM capability to create, edit, extend, and deploy copilots with ease of use, better results, and cost-effectiveness.
results.Feature details:
Solution management in Copilot Studio brings Power Platform application lifecycle management (ALM) directly into the Copilot Studio app.

When you create an agent in Copilot Studio, you add it to a Power Platform solution. With Power Platform ALM, you can create custom solutions to manage your agents across multiple environments. You can also use pipeline deployments and other ALM scenarios in the Power Platform admin center.

By adding solution management directly into the Copilot Studio app, you can view, export, and import solutions. You can set your preferred or default environment, configure pipelines, and customize and manage your solutions without leaving your agent-building environment.

The solution manager in Copilot Studio enables you to:

  • Create agents with solutions.
  • Set the preferred solution in Copilot Studio.
  • Create agents and components with a preferred solution.
  • Edit and extend agents and components with a preferred solution.
  • Deploy agents and components by using pipelines in Power Platform with a single click.
  • Share agents and components with a user or team.

For more information about the solution manager, including how to use it and how it connects to your broader organizational ALM scenarios, see Create and manage solutions in Copilot Studio.
results.Enabled for: Admins, makers, marketers, or analysts, automatically

results.Feature name: Add copilots to Teams meetings
results.Business value: Use Copilot Studio to create a custom copilot that works as an autonomous assistant to help you be more productive at work.
results.Feature details:
When you invite a copilot to your meeting in Teams, you can interact with it through text, speech, and video. The copilot uses generative AI and considers the entire audio conversation, chat transcript, and everything shared on screen to help you get more from your meetings.

You can give the copilot meeting commands, such as "Stop recording." You can ask about content, like "What was the highest value account in the shared spreadsheet?" You can also request contextually relevant analysis that draws on world knowledge, such as "Review this code and suggest improvements" or "What does this chart imply about the trend of this stock?".
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts

results.Feature name: Analyze the business impact of agents in Viva Insights
results.Business value: When business executives use this report in Viva Insights, they see aggregated, cross-tenant visibility to the business impact and value from using conversational agents across their company. The report makes it easier for executives to identify the return on investment and align agent usage with their business goals.
results.Feature details:
You can now analyze the business impact of your Microsoft Copilot Studio agents directly in Viva Insights. This feature gives you a consolidated report that shows usage and business impact trends across all your published Copilot Studio agents. You see key stats like hours saved, usage analysis, and resolution rate at the tenant level. You can drill down to view stats for each agent. The report uses regular usage data that Copilot Studio sends to Microsoft 365.

By default, this feature is enabled if you meet the following criteria:

  • You have at least 50 Microsoft 365 Copilot licenses (standalone or bundled with Viva Insights).
  • You publish at least one Copilot Studio agent in a production environment.

You control the feature in the Power Platform admin center. The feature is automatically enabled for all public customers in a commercial cloud when the criteria are met.

Users with the Insights Analyst role in Microsoft 365 can view the Copilot Studio agent report in Viva Insights.

Environment admins can turn off the report from the Power Platform admin center.

If your Copilot Studio data is in a different geographic location or region from your Microsoft 365 tenant, an environment admin must turn on data sharing across geographic boundaries.
results.Enabled for: Admins, makers, marketers, or analysts, automatically

results.Feature name: Add suggested prompts to custom copilots
results.Business value: When end users don't see the value of the copilot extension, conversational AI solutions fall short. Suggested prompts help users quickly see the value of the Copilot Studio.
results.Feature details:
When you create a copilot extension, you can add suggested prompts that the copilot supports.

For Microsoft 365 Chat experiences, the suggested prompts appear in the center of the page. Each prompt includes a title and description. The prompts rotate and appear prominently at the start of each session.

For other channels, users see this functionality in a way that fits the experiences for that channel.
results.Enabled for: Admins, makers, marketers, or analysts, automatically

results.Feature name: Connect to the range of knowledge sources in Copilot Studio
results.Business value: When makers integrate enterprise data into custom copilots, users can efficiently access relevant business insights. This integration enhances decision-making and productivity within the organization.
results.Feature details:
This feature enables makers to enrich their custom copilots with knowledge sources.

To get enterprise value from AI, customers need to integrate enterprise knowledge with foundational models. This integration includes enterprise data, data in application silos, and the Microsoft 365 Graph. Custom copilots that have knowledge make it easy to ground LLM responses in enterprise data. They give users relevant business insights in a secure and compliant way.

Supported types are public websites, SharePoint and OneDrive, file uploads, Dataverse (including Dynamics 365 and Power Apps data), and other external data sources indexed in Microsoft Graph.
