results.Feature name: Access real-time data during Teams meetings
results.Business value: During customer meetings, you can easily access the most important contextual CRM information and real-time tips in the new unified Copilot for Sales side panel in Teams.
results.Feature details:
• Unifying tabs in the Copilot for Sales side panel in Teams gives you a simpler and seamless way to access contextual CRM information during customer meetings.
  • Unified tabs provide a foundation for better integration with Microsoft Copilot experiences.
results.Enabled for: Users, automatically

results.Feature name: Save AI meeting notes into CRM from post-meeting card
results.Business value: Eliminate the hassle of manually logging meeting notes. Copilot for Sales streamlines post-meeting workflows by letting you save AI-generated meeting summaries directly into your CRM without switching context. This feature ensures that you effortlessly document critical insights, reduces administrative tasks, and helps sales teams stay focused on closing deals.
results.Feature details:
Copilot for Sales delivers a post-meeting notification card in Teams. The card gives you a concise summary of your customer meeting and seamless follow-up actions. The notification includes:

  • Meeting highlights – AI-generated summary that captures key discussion points.
  • Direct link to Teams recap – Access to the full meeting recording, transcript, and detailed insights.
  • One-click CRM save – Ability to save AI-generated meeting notes directly into your CRM, such as Salesforce, without switching apps.

When you add the Copilot for Sales app to your meeting and record and transcribe the meeting, you get the post-meeting notification card after your meeting ends.

With this feature, sales professionals can quickly capture and save key insights in CRM. This capability ensures that every conversation contributes to a well-documented and actionable sales pipeline.
results.Enabled for: Users, automatically

results.Feature name: Seamlessly capture sales meetings with automatic recordings
results.Business value: Ensure you capture every crucial detail in customer engagements. This feature automatically records and transcribes meetings, giving sellers complete records for comprehensive analysis. When your sales team uses these insights, they can streamline processes, reduce time spent on retrospective tasks, and focus on building stronger customer relationships. With this capability, your organization can optimize interactions and efficiently drive business success.
results.Feature details: Copilot for Sales automatically handles your meeting recordings, so you never miss a moment. As an administrator, you can turn on this feature for your organization through the Copilot for Sales admin settings page. When you turn on the feature, it applies to all users. Any meeting with Copilot for Sales added automatically records the meeting for the seller. Sellers don't need any extra setup. The feature automatically starts recordings for eligible meetings. Copilot for Sales doesn't record meetings that you mark as private.
results.Enabled for: Users, admins, makers, or analysts

results.Feature name: Generate post-meeting summary emails using AI for customer follow-up
results.Business value: Following up after meetings takes a lot of time and effort, especially when you create summarized notes to send to customers. With this feature, you can send a follow-up email with a single click by using high-quality AI-generated meeting notes, making post-meeting follow-up easy.
results.Feature details:
This release for Copilot for Sales enhances your productivity by improving the thank-you email generated after a Teams meeting:

  • Enhanced email draft quality: AI-generated email drafts better align with the meeting action items and notes, with an improved tone for effective customer communications.
  • Key details from the meeting: The solution automatically adds info such as participants’ email addresses to the To and Cc fields, streamlining the steps for sellers to just click Send.
  • Improved email draft management experience: The email draft opens in the seller’s default email client, which makes it easier to quickly edit and send.
results.Enabled for: Users, automatically

results.Feature name: Adding questions, keywords, and associated browsing in Teams meeting recap
results.Business value: Reach points of interest in seconds instead of having to go through hours of listening to audio and reading transcripts. In addition, aggregate these data points for benchmarking, comparisons, and trend analysis.
results.Feature details:
The Copilot for Sales post-meeting summary, integrated within a Teams meeting recap, serves as a convenient hub for sellers to review their meetings with customers. It simplifies the process of updating their CRM or following up with customers, ensuring they stay informed and responsive to customer needs and inquiries.

Sellers can access the following capabilities from the Teams Recap tab:

  • Sales explicit keywords and implicit mentions: Tracked keywords that were defined by the organizations and out-of-the-box mentions such as brands, people, and time.
  • Sales questions: Questions that were asked by participants (customers and sellers) during the meeting.

Sellers can select points of interest and reach them in the recording timeline.

results.Feature name: Collaborate with sales teams using AI-powered planner tasks
results.Business value:
Sales teams are highly collaborative and spend a significant amount of time communicating and coordinating with colleagues within Microsoft Teams. During your communication in Teams, you discuss important decisions and action items. However, the manual process of creating and tracking tasks from these discussions is time-consuming and can lead to missed tasks.

To address the challenges you and your sales teams face, use an intelligent and automated task-creation solution in Teams channels where you install the Copilot for Sales app. This solution streamlines task management, improves collaboration, and enhances productivity for you and your team.
results.Feature details:
• Automated prompting: The Copilot for Sales bot in Teams channels automatically scans conversations for relevant discussions and action items. When it identifies potential tasks, it prompts you and other sales team members with a notification, seeking your agreement to create the tasks.
  • Task creation and assignment: When you agree, the solution automatically creates Teams tasks (planner tasks) based on the identified action items. You see a preview of the tasks. The solution assigns the tasks to relevant users to ensure accountability.
  • Planner app integration in Teams: Sales team members get a centralized view of all the tasks by pinning the planner app to the Teams channel. Users can also access these tasks from the planner app.

This solution works for both Dynamics 365 and Salesforce CRM users.

results.Feature name: Add tags to meetings to easily search for them
results.Business value: Tagging in meeting summaries allows team members to quickly locate and reference specific discussions, streamlining the process of accessing vital meeting information. The use of tags organizes meeting content effectively, enabling you to sort and focus on summaries that are most pertinent to their needs. This organization aids in managing the wealth of information generated from meetings, making it more manageable and useful.
results.Feature details: After a meeting ends, you can add tags to the meeting. These tags are saved against the meeting so you can search for them. When searching for a tag, all meetings with that tag are shown in the search results, making it easy for sellers and managers to find relevant conversations.

results.Feature name: Start your day with a Copilot for Sales meeting summary
results.Business value:
The daily meeting prep notification helps you work more efficiently by giving you one summary of your upcoming sales meetings each day. This feature shows key insights in advance, including participant details, strategic context, and next-best actions. It removes the need for manual preparation, saves time, and helps you enter each conversation with confidence and clarity.

For the business, this feature boosts seller productivity by cutting down on context switching and fragmented preparation efforts. It leads to higher-quality customer interactions, better pipeline progression, and deal conversion. It also lowers operational overhead from ad hoc meeting prep tools or fragmented CRM usage.

Admin-level configurability lets you align the feature with your organizational rhythms and helps prevent notification fatigue. The feature still delivers critical insights that drive better outcomes.
results.Feature details:
A daily meeting prep notification gives sellers a summary of their upcoming meetings in a structured format. The daily card holds key insights for the next five sales meetings, organized by meeting date. Each meeting shows the title, agenda, date and time, and meeting length. Sellers can select a link to open the Teams meeting. A link to help prepare for the meeting sends a new adaptive card with pre-meeting details for that meeting.

Admins set the daily time for sending the meeting digest. They can also turn the digest on or off for the whole environment.
results.Enabled for: Users, automatically

results.Feature name: Access and analyze sales data with Sales Chat
results.Business value: Sales Chat simplifies information retrieval and consumption. Sellers spend less time searching for information in different applications, combining information, and trying to derive insights. They can conveniently use natural language to find the right information across applications, analyze the information, and derive insights—all from within Sales Chat.
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts

results.Feature name: Configure copilot features and customize settings
results.Business value: Copilot for Service gives your admins the flexibility to enable and customize copilot features to suit your agents' needs.
results.Feature details:
Admins use the Copilot for Service app to access these capabilities:

  • Admins with at least conditional access can turn on or off Copilot features. They can also give access to these features to specific users.
  • Environment admins can turn on or off Copilot features for each environment.
  • Environment admins can customize CRM entities and set which fields users can edit.

results.Feature name: Deploy Copilot for Service app to Outlook and Teams
results.Business value: When administrators with at least conditional access deploy Copilot for Service in modern business apps, agents can more easily get the information they need, when they need it.
results.Feature details:
• An admin with at least conditional access can deploy the Copilot for Service app in the Microsoft admin center for both Outlook and Teams.
  • The admin can specify the users who have access to the app.
  • When you deploy this app, you enable Copilot for Service in Outlook and Teams. It provides service-specific copilot capabilities.

results.Feature name: Enable ServiceNow integration in Outlook and Teams
results.Business value: With the ServiceNow integration, admins can bring data from ServiceNow into Copilot for Service. Users can see integrated data from multiple CRM sources in Outlook and Teams, so they can access the latest case information for customers.
results.Feature details:
With the ServiceNow integration, admins can take the following actions:

  • Sign in to the ServiceNow CRM system in the Copilot for Service app.

results.Feature name: Plugins support for Copilot embedded in third-party CRMs
results.Business value:
This feature empowers businesses that use third-party CRM solutions to leverage the capabilities of Copilot for seamless operations. By enabling AI-powered insights and automation, organizations can:

  • Enhance customer relationship management.
  • Automate repetitive tasks to save time and reduce errors.
  • Gain actionable insights to improve decision-making tasks.
  • Provide faster and more personalized responses to customers.
results.Feature details:
You can now embed Copilot into third-party CRM systems to help your business streamline operations and deliver better customer experiences. Key functionalities include:

Data integration: Copilot securely connects to third-party system data. Users can query and retrieve information like customer details, order history, or case status.

Seamless setup: Use plugins to easily configure and extend Copilot within non-Microsoft CRM environments. Admins can set up the plugins easily through the admin center.
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts

results.Feature name: Use Copilot-generated prompts in your third-party CRM
results.Business value: This feature streamlines how customer service representatives interact with Copilot. By offering relevant prompt suggestions based on the case or conversation context, it enables faster and more accurate responses. By reducing the time spent on manual input, the feature helps improve user efficiency and customer satisfaction. This improvement leads to a higher engagement rate and more effective case management in supported CRM systems.
results.Feature details:
When you use Copilot in your CRM solution, it generates prompts based on the context of the active case or conversation. Copilot creates prompts for only the first question, not for follow-up questions.

  • For a case: Copilot looks at the title, description, notes, and linked emails within the case in the CRM solution.
  • For a conversation: Copilot uses the intent from the first three messages to create a prompt. It refreshes the prompts for every third message. Users can select a prompt to generate a response.

Turn on this feature in the admin center so customer service representatives receive prompts that help them improve their use of Copilot for Service.
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts

results.Feature name: Embed case summary customizations in your CRM system
results.Business value: When admins customize case summaries, they can include only the relevant fields from third-party CRM systems. This flexibility improves case summary accuracy, customer service representative efficiency, and alignment with organizational workflows.
results.Feature details:
When you embed this feature in third-party CRM systems, Copilot supports customized case summary fields through a pro-code approach. Admins can change the adaptor to set the case fields that Copilot includes in the case summary.

This feature release includes the following capabilities:

Field flexibility: Supports all standard and custom case fields in third-party CRM systems. Improved summaries: Lets you tailor case summaries so service representatives see only relevant information. This customization reduces noise and boosts efficiency. CRM options: By using a pro-code approach to customize the case summary, this feature works with almost any custom CRM system. This support brings a powerful capability to a wide range of customers.
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts

results.Feature name: Let agents use Copilot when drafting emails to customers
results.Business value: When Copilot helps agents draft emails, they spend less time authoring messages and more time helping customers solve complex issues. By providing the best possible content, Copilot for Service helps agents increase productivity.
results.Feature details:
Copilot for Service helps customers compose emails and respond to emails by using the context of customer records and related actions, such as cases and emails. By offering optimized content suggestions, Copilot for Service helps improve agent productivity.

When you deploy this feature in Salesforce or ServiceNow, you get the following functionality:

  • Agents can select from example queries that Copilot provides.
  • Agents can choose whether to use knowledge articles to generate the email draft.
  • Agents can start a new query, and Copilot creates an email draft.
  • Agents can copy the email draft that Copilot creates and paste it into the body of the email.
  • Agents can edit a Copilot draft before copying it into the body of the email.
  • Agents can adjust the tone of the email content as needed.

Admins must enable this feature for agents to use it.

results.Feature name: Obtain advanced reconciliation insights and suggestions
results.Business value:
Finance agents give finance professionals more time by bringing insights that support strategic decision-making and reduce time spent on manual, repetitive work.

Through AI-driven analysis of data structures and suggested reconciliation rules, the Financial Reconciliation Agent gives finance teams a sophisticated but user-friendly tool that improves the accuracy and efficiency of reconciling financial data.
results.Feature details:
With the advanced reconciliation insights and suggestions feature, you can efficiently access actionable suggestions to reduce unmatched transactions and improve matching outcomes. These insights use existing capabilities such as data preparation to offer context-specific recommendations. They highlight the potential impact of each suggestion. You can implement improvements directly and take targeted actions to enhance reconciliation results. This feature helps you greatly reduce the time and effort needed to resolve unmatched transactions. With this feature, you can:

  • Review a summary of the matching process, including analytics. The feature suggests actions and next steps to help reduce unmatched transactions and improve matched results.
  • Analyze pre-reconciliation and post-reconciliation suggestions.
  • Get suggestions based on an analysis of the formatting and structure of source data. You also get suggestions based on selected reconciliation parameters.
  • Take action directly from the suggestions.
results.Enabled for: Admins, makers, or analysts enable for users

results.Feature name: Collaborate and share out of variance analyses in Excel
results.Business value: Microsoft 365 Copilot for Finance brings productivity to a new level when you analyze variances. Collaborate with others during the process, and easily share your results in a presentation-ready format.

results.Feature name: Apply partial matching to reconcile financial data
results.Business value:
Microsoft Copilot for Finance uses partial matching methods to make its data structure comparisons more precise and sophisticated.

This feature improves accuracy and simplifies transaction categorization. It reduces unmatched transactions and more effectively highlights potential matches.
results.Feature details:
Microsoft Copilot for Finance uses a partial matching technique alongside exact matching to identify not only exact matches but also similar values within your data. Specifically, Copilot for Finance uses exact substring matching that enables it to recognize occurrences of one string (substring) within another string (superset) precisely as they appear.

This feature is particularly beneficial for mapping keys used in the reconciliation process. Copilot for Finance accurately identifies and categorizes partially matching transactions, presenting them within the existing "Potentially matched transactions" group in your reconciliation report.
results.Enabled for: Users, automatically
results.GeographicAreasDetails:
This feature is available in the following Microsoft Azure geographic area:

  • United States

results.Feature name: Incorporate supplementary data
results.Business value: This feature improves decision-making by providing comprehensive insights through supplementary content combined with the core dataset. With this feature, you save time by uncovering extra variance details without needing a secondary analysis.
results.Feature details:
To identify extra factors that influence financial variances, the agent uses supplementary data. The agent suggests relevant data to reference, incorporates that data into the analysis, and identifies insights. With this feature, you can:

  • Enter supplementary data for each criterion in the variance analysis. You can add data for different levels in the process.
  • Enter structured supplementary data, like Excel tables. You can add extra facts to the existing workbook where you do the variance analysis.
  • Enter unstructured supplementary data, like external documents. You store these documents outside the workbook.
  • Get a references section for the analysis output. The section includes proper citations for references in the updated analysis summary.
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts

results.Feature name: Create user-defined classification rules
results.Business value: This feature provides flexibility to support multiple types of reconciliations driven by user needs or company policies. With this feature, reconciliation reporting classifies transactions in the match category and gives context for each transaction. The classification rules vary based on customer needs, reconciliation type (such as intercompany, subledger, or tax), and the specific reconciliation parameter selections that you need for each reconciliation type.
results.Feature details:
When you set up reconciliation, specify how to categorize different matching relationships in the reconciliation report. These relationships include one-to-many, many-to-many, and many-to-one relationships. You decide whether each type of match is matched or potentially matched. Customize the classification to fit your preferences and business requirements for different types of financial reconciliations. You can create criteria for classifying matched results in the reconciliation report by using combinations of mapping key modifiers.

  • Choose how to classify one-to-many, many-to-many, or many-to-one matches during the reconciliation setup process.
  • Create customizable sub-classifications within matching groups.
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts

results.Feature name: Perform advanced troubleshooting
results.Business value: This feature supports the iterative nature of the reconciliation process. It helps resolve unmatched transactions so users save time and directly share and collaborate with other team members while troubleshooting.
results.Feature details:
Reconciliation is often an iterative process. It can require collaboration with other finance team members and deep investigation to uncover root causes behind unmatched transactions. This process ensures thorough and accurate reconciliation. With this feature, you can:

  • Share transaction and related transaction troubleshooting information through email or Teams. Related transactions include one-to-many, many-to-many, and many-to-one transactions.
  • Create comments and tag other users to transactions you're reviewing through the troubleshooting capability.
  • Export or print troubleshooting information to a report.
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts

results.Feature name: Use Excel to analyze variances
results.Business value:
With Microsoft 365 Copilot for Finance, you can analyze variances seamlessly through the familiar Microsoft Excel interface. Define precise criteria for discrepancies, use extra data sources for targeted analysis, and easily create detailed variance analysis reports with comments that improve communication with stakeholders.

Regularly explore your organization's core by examining forecast-to-actual trends. Stay ahead and keep all stakeholders updated on your business's changing state with timely, insightful analyses. Enjoy many benefits:

  • Reduce risk: Use intelligent anomaly detection to lower risk and proactively resolve potential issues. Protect your organization's financial health.
  • Accelerate decision-making: Streamline your analysis cycle to enable fast, informed decisions. With Copilot's advanced features, you speed up your path from data to actionable insights.
  • Save time: Say goodbye to tedious manual commentary and reporting processes. Copilot automates these tasks and delivers accurate insights from a wide range of data records. With Copilot, you save valuable time to focus on strategic initiatives.
results.Feature details:
Microsoft 365 Copilot for Finance supports the following capabilities for variance analysis tasks:

  • Set criteria for finding variances in the Excel spreadsheet that need extra analysis. You can set these criteria as natural language instructions right in the Excel spreadsheet. Copilot uses these criteria to find and highlight variances in the worksheet.
  • Review the variance analysis results that Copilot generates. You can adjust or add to these results.
  • Report variance analysis results in a form that's ready to use.

results.Feature name: Use Copilot extended reconciliation parametrization
results.Business value: With Microsoft 365 Copilot for Finance, you can tailor the reconciliation process by categorizing transactions to improve comparison accuracy. This customization lets you match your specific requirements and the state of the data you reconcile. You maintain clarity in the reconciliation reporting by defining how the data is presented.
results.Feature details:
Microsoft 365 Copilot for Finance introduces enhanced capabilities for the reconciliation process, offering the following features:

  • Customizable tolerance definition: Tailor your reconciliation to your business process specifics by defining allowed tolerance levels. This feature empowers you to categorize transactions accurately, ensuring alignment with your unique operational requirements.

  • Numeric and monetary value parameterization: Take full control of numeric and monetary values with Copilot's parametrization options. Whether it's adjusting values to absolute for comparison or modifying signs, you can customize your settings to match your exact needs and the state of the raw data.

  • Precision and presentation settings: Fine-tune your reconciliation with configurable floating precision, rounding rules, and data presentation options. This level of parametrization allows you to maintain consistency and clarity in your financial reporting.

You can enjoy uninterrupted workflow continuity while maximizing productivity and ensuring timely reconciliation updates. results.Feature name: Perform AI-driven multilevel contextual analyses
results.Business value: With this feature, you can get the analysis and output you want more efficiently because you don't need to specify details upfront. The agent understands data dependencies and creates an analysis plan that shows the detail and drill-down levels needed for a meaningful result. By following the analysis, you can use simple, interactive prompts to have conversations with the agent and get more insights into the analysis and findings.
results.Feature details:
For an efficient and effective variance analysis, the agent can:

  • Create a plan that outlines dependencies and required analysis levels to systematically identify areas that need attention. The plan also specifies the depth of analysis for each criterion or segments within the criteria.
  • Engage in natural-language AI-driven interactions and conversations that span multiple exchanges and levels. These interactions let the agent maintain context as it iterates through the analysis.
  • Prompt you to provide more context, clarification, and detail. These prompts help the agent gather comprehensive information and refine its approach to deliver a higher-value analysis and insights.
  • Accelerate the discovery of meaningful trends and variances. The agent tailors the analytical experience with each interaction to support informed decision-making across complex financial domains.

results.Feature name: Boost collections efficiency with the Collections Agent
results.Business value: Finance agents in Outlook optimize the collections experience and foster efficiency and accuracy in accounts receivable operations. Users can connect to their ERP system directly from Outlook to easily access valuable customer accounts and customer receivables insights from their customer data in the ERP system. They get help when they craft email responses and save communication summaries and action items for a greater impact.
results.Feature details:
When you integrate this feature with Outlook, you get a Copilot assistive experience that focuses on accounts receivable. This feature improves customer communication and the collections process. It helps reduce the time it takes to collect outstanding receivables and improves communication with customers. Several key features help you:

  • Get a summary of customer information directly from the source enterprise resource planning (ERP) system. You don't need to manually retrieve data.
  • Create customer communication emails that focus on the collections process.
  • Create, read, and share relevant notes, details, and related emails for the collections process for a specific customer.
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts

results.Feature name: Use Copilot for Finance additional native ERP support
results.Business value:
Microsoft Copilot for Finance revolutionizes the collections experience for accounts receivable (AR) personnel. By integrating with Outlook, it enhances customer communications through several key features:

  • Provides a summary of customer information directly from the source ERP, so you don't need to manually retrieve data.
  • Offers a summarization of inbound emails in Outlook, streamlining the communication process.
  • Assists with crafting customer communication emails, ensuring clarity and professionalism.
  • Serves as a single source of truth storage in the ERP for critical updates such as transactions, contact information, action items, and relevant customer communication.

This holistic approach not only optimizes the collections experience but also fosters efficiency and accuracy in AR operations.

Steady and predictable cash flow is essential for a healthy business. It provides the necessary liquidity for day-to-day operations and supports strategic investments for growth. An effective collections process in accounts receivable plays a critical role in this context. It ensures timely revenue inflow and drives the predictability that financial planning and stability require.

Accounts receivable professionals spend a lot of time in Microsoft Outlook responding to customer balance inquiries. They switch between Outlook and ERP to gather the details they need to do their work. They update ERP with information such as follow-up actions, updated contact information, and promise-to-pay dates.

Microsoft Copilot for Finance streamlines this process. It enables employees to connect directly to their ERP system from within Outlook. While communicating with customers, accounts receivable personnel can access valuable ERP customer data, get help crafting email responses, and conveniently save communication summaries and action items back in the ERP system—all while avoiding the toll of switching apps.
results.Feature details:
Copilot for Finance expands its native support for additional ERPs by introducing Dynamics 365 Business Central and SAP. Along with the generative AI features for email summarization and composition, this integration enhances the collection coordinator experience in Microsoft Outlook by offering a range of features that include:

  • Customer Financial Details Retrieval: The add-in enables users to access and retrieve customer financial details directly within Outlook, streamlining the process of financial management and decision-making.
  • Contact and Invoice Updates: Users can update contact information and invoices directly from Outlook, ensuring that all data is current and accurate across all platforms.
  • Business Document Management: The add-in supports the ability to download and send business documents such as invoices and balance statements. This feature simplifies the process of document management and distribution, saving time and improving efficiency.

Simplifying support for extensibility lets you customize the application to fit your unique needs. This flexibility improves business outcomes and enhances performance. It enables more thorough end-to-end scenario completion and better workflows, which streamlines business operations.

results.Feature name: Manage accounts receivable in Teams
results.Business value:
Collections software plays an important role in modern business operations. It streamlines the process of recovering outstanding debts. Some of the major advantages include the following benefits:

  • Streamlined workflows: Effective collections software brings all key data elements into one view. It empowers you to execute key end-to-end processes without switching between multiple applications.
  • Enhanced communication: By providing a centralized platform for managing emails and phone calls, you can ensure all interactions are tracked and accessible by authorized personnel. This approach promotes increased transparency.
  • Increased compliance: Microsoft 365 Copilot for Finance is built on compliant, secure platform technology. With this technology, you can better comply with industry regulations and legal requirements.
  • Reporting and analytics: Copilot for Finance gives accounts receivable managers insights into team performance, collection trends, and customer behavior. With these insights, managers can better measure the effectiveness of their copilot initiatives. This data-driven approach helps teams find areas for improvement, optimize strategies, and make smarter business decisions.

Collections teams see benefits when they use these tools:

  • Lower days sales outstanding (DSOs): Collections teams lower DSOs when they quickly respond to customer inquiries. They avoid delays when customers ask for key documents.
  • Lower delinquency rates: Collections software sends automated reminders and follow-up tasks. It cuts down the time it takes to collect debts. This faster process brings in cash sooner and improves financial performance.
  • Better resource productivity: Collections software automates routine tasks and makes communication easier. Agents can handle more cases and resolve them faster. This boost helps the whole team's productivity.
  • Improved customer service: Collections software helps you create a positive customer experience by making it easy to send personalized messages and resolve questions quickly.
results.Feature details:
Copilot for Finance gives collections coordinators a new set of capabilities that streamlines and improves the collections process. Copilot for Finance provides an immersive experience in Microsoft Teams that offers the following capabilities to reduce days of outstanding sales and improve cash flow.

  • Worklist items: This feature shows the list of cases that collections coordinators need to work on. The list sorts cases by priority, urgency, and status. Each item includes details about the customer, the invoice, and the relevant correspondence details.
  • Pre-call preparation: With generative AI, collections coordinators get call scripts to help them prepare for calls and support customers during calls.
  • Copilot dialing support: During customer calls, collections coordinators can interact with Copilot for Finance to get specific customer insights and take action on key scenarios. These scenarios typically require context switching to the ERP.
  • Post-call support: This feature stores call summaries, transcripts, and calls in Microsoft Dataverse. It also recommends the next best actions.

results.Feature name: Use Copilot for Finance macroeconomic analysis
results.Business value:
With Microsoft 365 Copilot for Finance, corporate financial leaders use generative AI to create meaningful, timely summaries of macroeconomic conditions. The AI draws from multiple selective market data sources to help leaders optimize their investment and business decisions. Ever-changing conditions require consistent monitoring of various market-driving areas and themes that affect capital investments.

Creating impactful analyses means reviewing a large volume of content from different geographies, themes, industries, government sectors, and market indices. Collaborating on this effort can be challenging when you need to deliver effective content within a limited decision-making window.

This effort can limit your sources, slow your reaction to sudden macroeconomic events, and make it hard to monitor trends and keep stakeholders informed in real time.

Copilot for Finance helps you broaden your sources, deliver more refined insights, and stay informed in real time. Copilot for Finance optimizes how you monitor macroeconomic signals. With the collaborative nature of Teams, Copilot for Finance creates macroeconomic, topic-driven libraries that treasury teams and senior financial leaders can easily contribute to and access. A team's experience also gives you a centralized space for stakeholder reports, general updates on macroeconomic conditions, and late-breaking news that impacts capital markets.
results.Feature details:
Copilot for Finance supports the following capabilities for macroeconomic analysis capabilities:

  • Finance team collaboration on macroeconomic topics.
  • Customizable library of macroeconomic topics.
  • AI that monitors and summarizes external macroeconomic signals.
  • Preparation and sharing of stakeholder reports on macroeconomic trends.

results.Feature name: Support additional data storage needs
results.Business value: Enable users to seamlessly manage extensive healthcare-specific workloads by leveraging the Fast Healthcare Interoperability Resources (FHIR) service.
results.Feature details:
Azure Health Data Services is a cloud-based solution that helps you collect, store, and analyze health data from different sources and formats. It supports various healthcare standards, such as FHIR and Digital Imaging and Communications in Medicine (DICOM), and converts data from legacy or proprietary device formats to FHIR.

With this feature enhancement, Azure Health Data Services enables you to:

  • Connect health data from different systems, devices, and types in one place.
  • Discover new insights from health data using machine learning, analytics, and AI tools.
  • Build on a trusted cloud that protects your health data and meets privacy and compliance requirements.
  • Meet your current health data needs and adapt to future developments. Azure Health Data Services is a powerful and flexible solution that is always evolving. We're continuously improving and updating the platform to support new and emerging health data standards, so you don't have to worry about changing your data formats or systems in the future.
  • Store data up to 100 TB using the Azure SQL Database Hyperscale tier.
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts

results.Feature name: Support availability zones for Azure Health Data Services
results.Business value: Enhance customer resilience and satisfaction by implementing a voluntary program for disaster recovery assistance. Database backups are an essential part of any business continuity and disaster recovery strategy since they help protect customer data from corruption or deletion. Data redundancy in backup and disaster recovery strategy stores multiple copies of customer data so that data is protected from planned and unplanned events. These events include transient hardware failures, network or power outages, and massive natural disasters.
results.Feature details: This feature provides availability zone support for FHIR services in Azure Health Data Services in order to maintain business continuity and offer data recovery options such as backup and data replication. Azure availability zones enable data redundancy by providing unique physical locations within an Azure region. Availability zone readiness is a general availability (GA) requirement for all Azure products to meet Azure customer promises. They help your data stay synchronized and accessible if things go wrong.
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts

results.Feature name: Use DICOM service with Azure Data Lake integration
results.Business value: Integration with Azure Data Lake empowers customers with complete control over imaging data, enhancing flexibility in accessing and working with that data using the Azure Storage ecosystem and APIs. Furthermore, Data Lake Storage also serves as a connection point to various services, including Microsoft Fabric, unlocking fresh opportunities for analytics and AI with medical imaging data.
results.Feature details:
The current architecture of the Digital Imaging and Communications in Medicine (DICOM) service automatically provisions an Azure Storage account in the background to store uploaded DICOM data. This storage account is only accessible via the DICOM web APIs, and its security and lifecycle are solely managed by the DICOM service.

In the new architecture, users have the flexibility to specify their own fully managed Azure Data Lake Storage account. This user-managed storage account will be utilized by the DICOM service to store DICOM data received via the DICOM web APIs. Similar to other services or applications accessing data in a storage account, the DICOM service will be granted access to this data. Users can revoke this access at any time without impacting their ability to access data.

Using Azure Data Lake Storage with the DICOM service offers several benefits to users:

  • Enables direct access to DICOM data stored by the DICOM service using Azure Storage APIs, alongside DICOM web APIs, providing increased flexibility for data access and usage.
  • Introduces DICOM data to the broader ecosystem of tools designed for working with Azure Storage, including AzCopy, Azure Storage Explorer, and the Azure Storage Data Movement library.
  • Unlocks new possibilities for analytics and AI/machine learning scenarios by seamlessly integrating with Azure Data Lake Storage. This includes compatibility with services such as Azure Synapse Analytics, Azure Databricks, Azure Machine Learning, and Microsoft Fabric.
  • Provides users with controls to manage storage permissions, access controls, tiers, and rules.
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts

This step enables team members in the factory to use it as an assistant for data-related and insight-related questions, significantly increasing their efficiency for data-intensive tasks like root-cause analysis.

results.Feature name: Manage Store operations tasks in Teams
results.Business value:
Managing tasks daily is a busy operational practice for retail managers. Tasks can be diverse and on different platforms, which brings even more accountability to the front-line worker to ensure that their tasks for the day are executed at the required time in the expected way, across different applications.

To make the journey of front-line workers easier, seamless, and efficient, this feature gives a common platform for managing all tasks under a single umbrella of Microsoft Teams.
results.Feature details:
Microsoft Teams serves as the centralized platform for managing all your tasks by seamlessly integrating with Store Operations Assist. Now, you can conveniently access and oversee all tasks within the unified interface of Microsoft Teams.

This functionality empowers you to efficiently execute specific Store operations tasks directly from the Microsoft Teams task window. With a simple click on the task, you can open a detailed view, respond to survey questions, and fulfill task requirements effortlessly. The synchronization of task status ensures real-time updates, enabling you to track the progress of any task with ease through the Teams interface.

results.Feature name: Enable store employees to move between stores
results.Business value: Retail is a fast-paced environment and experiences high employee turnover rates, which poses a challenge to manage store tasks and assist customers. Often store employees must work in more than one store to support critical store tasks or fill in for someone. At this point, it becomes important that store associates can make a smooth transition between stores (in the Store Operation Assist application) and access their relevant task-related data. With this feature in place, store employees could assist store operations in other stores as well seamlessly.
results.Feature details:
With this functionality, a store manager would be able to oversee task management in more than one store. A store associate would be able to temporarily work out of a peer store, when there’s a shortage of staff, or a store needs extra hands to manage special events.

This feature enables support for matrix data structure and providing the store associate with the right set of user permissions and correct security role. Additionally, modifications are made in Store Operations Assist admin to support correct task ownership and assignment to the business unit. This capability allows store associates to access any business unit's data regardless of what business unit the store associate is assigned to.

results.Feature name: Personalize product discovery
results.Business value:
Personalization that has been synonymous with customer engagements only, holds a totally new meaning in the agentic world. A personalized product discovery and shopping experience holds as much true for an in-store store associate as much it holds true for a customer accessing details via customer website. 

With customers expecting seamless and personalized shopping experiences while retailers, especially in-store, are grappling with operational inefficiencies, labor shortages, fluctuating demand, increasing competition, there is a huge need for tool that can ensure all the product related information in very easily accessible way to help close the deals in the most conducive and short possible time
results.Feature details:
Personalized product discovery based on Copilot Studio, is an experience that is headless in nature i.e. it can be embedded into any user interface and can work across multiple personas and use cases. It can be enabled for a B2B (store associate) and a B2C (end customers) persona. 

The end consumers and store associates can now interact with the AI-enabled assistant, seek suggestions and guidance to help in making an informed shopping decision. The app helps to convert the interactions into an assisted sale closure in a seamless way.

The Copilot Studio-based agent provides a robust and scalable architecture that is easy to deploy and implement. Leveraging the out of box connectors and flow you can bring in the data from diverse core business applications and third parties to bring in a very personalized and contextual product discovery experience for your customers and store associates
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts

results.Feature name: Enable individually crafted responses
results.Investment area: Copilot template for personalized shopping enhancements
results.Business value:
Personalized shopping conversations focus on the shopper's immediate needs but without prior knowledge of their shopping history, the results are limited. However, integrating data from existing personalization and order history systems can enrich the conversation with insights about the shopper's preferences and buying habits.

Post-conversation, the chat session can be analyzed to further enhance the shopper's profile in these systems. This not only provides a more personalized shopping experience but also adds significant business value. It enables retailers to better understand their customers, tailor their offerings, and make more accurate product recommendations. This can lead to increased customer satisfaction, loyalty, and higher sales.
results.Feature details: With this feature, upon initial login, the system will present personalized prompts to the shopper. These may include suggestions to repurchase items or complementary products based on their previous purchases. Each recommendation will be customized according to the shopper's expressed preferences. The system's open interface is designed to integrate seamlessly with various third-party personalization tools, with some pre-integrated for immediate use.

The adoption of the copilot solution will become faster as the deployment journey is shortened by making this an embedded app rather than a separate app. For retailers, reducing the entry gate will result in a faster ROI realization.

The deployment will be made configurable with minimal clicks which can help customers with self-deployment post the installation of Sitecore OrderCloud-Fabric connector. results.Feature name: Enable plugin extensibility

Recognizing that some retailers have unique capabilities and processes they wish to offer their shoppers, such as detailed product information or interaction with a scheduling or service system, we've made it possible to integrate these extensions with specific intents in the shopping experience.

For instance, if a user expresses interest in scheduling an appointment, the system will automatically route this request to the appropriate plugin. This seamless integration not only enhances the user experience but also adds significant business value. It allows retailers to differentiate their offerings, improve customer engagement, and potentially increase sales by making their unique services more accessible and user-friendly.
results.Feature details: The plugins are integrated with the standard OpenAI plugin API. They can be built and deployed using a variety of tool sets, including Microsoft AI Studio and Copilot Studio. They are deployed independently of the copilot template so they can be managed independently. The user experience is seamless, so they do not know when their request is routed to a plugin.

results.Feature name: Use prebuilt industry templates
results.Investment area: Retail data solutions in Microsoft Fabric
results.Business value: In today’s fiercely competitive retail landscape, retailers strive to gain a profound understanding of their customers. Making data-driven decisions across all facets of their business is crucial. The presence of effective analytical solutions significantly contributes to achieving success. To allow retailers to enable these analytical solutions with reduced effort and faster time to market, we will be providing ready-made industry templates for the most widely used retail scenarios like inventory optimization analytics, customer segmentation, and customer sentiment analysis as part of this release. These templates will consist of a standardized data model, sample PBI report, and AI/ML (optional) capabilities embedded within each template.
results.Feature details:
We are investing in several key features and capabilities:

  • Automatic mapping and transformation pipeline in retail: This will allow retailers to streamline data integration, migration, and transformation. We will provide a smart matching feature which will take retailers source schema and based upon the industry template selected, we will auto-map source fields with target. This will significantly reduce the effort needed by each retailer to do this process manually. We will also generate the data pipelines or notebooks needed for doing the actual transformation.

  • Customer segmentation in retail: This involves segregating your customer base into distinct groups based on shared characteristics, behaviors, or demographics. The goal is to better understand and target specific segments with tailored marketing approaches that resonate with their unique needs and preferences. This helps with tailoring marketing strategies, inventory optimization, and using data to define various pricing strategies based upon your segments.

  • Inventory optimization in retail: This refers to the strategic management of inventory to achieve a right balance of supply and demand, reducing costs and increasing customer satisfaction. It involves forecasting the demand and inventory needs based upon historic sales trends, customer buying patterns etc. The correct inventory optimization solution has a huge impact on a company's top / bottom line.

  • Customer sentiment analysis: This is the ability to take the customer feedback to understand customer brand affinity and their purchasing patterns. It involves analyzing customer feedback through surveys, feedback, product reviews, market analysis etc. to understand their sentiment towards the product, brand, and company.

  • Supply chain analytics in retail: This refers to leveraging data and analytics to optimize various aspects related to procurement, production, distribution, and inventory management. Effective supply chain tracking helps retailers to reduce costs, optimize inventory, track supplier performance, and improve logistics.
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts

This data model allows retailers to store all their retail data in a single place, combine it to generate valuable insights and easily enable cross application workflows.

This capability will provide the ability for customers to install only the data model for the use cases that are enabled on top of the retail data solutions in Microsoft Fabric.

results.Feature name: Configure IDM extension with Sitecore OrderCloud connector
results.Investment area: Retail data solutions in Microsoft Fabric
results.Business value:
The key feature of the current connector is to provide a plug and play medium to bring retailer’s data from OrderCloud into Fabric. While the existing tables from OrderCloud and their respective mapping within the industry data model can work for a good number of our customers, we do understand that there will also be a need to bring additional tables or custom tables from the existing database (in OrderCloud) and map it to standard tables within the industry data model within Fabric for addressing business requirements. An easier, supportive handling of new table addition and respective mapping at this stage can reduce the manual work involved to a significant extent.

With this feature, we aim to minimize manual work for retailers and facilitate easy and rapid adoption, enabling them to bring their data into Fabric quickly.
results.Feature details: The feature will include a configurable approach toward adding new tables to the existing Sitecore OrderCloud connector within the retail data model. Retail data managers will be able to initiate the addition of tables through user-friendly forms and will get automated recommendations on the possible table mappings, which they can then choose and extend for their respective requirements, as required.
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts

results.Feature name: Enhance retail data solutions
results.Investment area: Retail data solutions in Microsoft Fabric
results.Business value:
We are committed to elevatvating retail data solutions by aligning it with Microsoft Fabric. As Fabric undergoes rapid advancements, our focus is to integrate these new capabilities into retail data solutions. This approach ensures that our customers consistently experience the latest features and innovations within the broader Microsoft ecosystem.

Our goal is to provide retail data solutions that reflect the latest from Microsoft Fabric but also offer a unique and enriched value proposition, positioning it as a standout solution in the realm of retail data management.
results.Feature details:
Our goal is to empower customers with the capability to seamlessly refresh their instances of retail data solutions with the latest industry data model. This enhancement ensures that our customers continually benefit from the most up-to-date improvements across multiple capabilities built on top of retail data solutions. We are also focusing on refining the management of multiple data managers to provide a more streamlined and efficient experience.

In addition to staying current with the industry's latest data model, we are committed to adapting and integrating the newest capabilities from Fabric onto retail data solutions. This approach ensures that our customers not only have access to cutting-edge features but also enjoy a more synchronized and integrated experience across the entire platform.

By facilitating these updates and improvements, our aim is to empower our customers to maintain a state-of-the-art and seamlessly managed environment, providing them with the tools they need to thrive in an ever-evolving retail landscape.
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts

results.Feature name: Use Sitecore OrderCloud Connector in retail data solutions
results.Business value:
Sitecore OrderCloud Connector, one of the key capabilities of retail data solutions, gives you a quick and reliable platform to get your commerce data into Microsoft retail industry standard schema.

The connector works across three key data sets:

  • Products: All the sellable products are available in Sitecore OrderCloud.
  • Customers: End users shopping from your eCommerce portals.
  • Orders: Sale orders created by customers.
results.Feature details:
Sitecore OrderCloud Connector, one of the key capabilities of Retail data solutions, gives you a quick and reliable platform to get your commerce data into Microsoft Retail industry standard schema. The connector performs transformation and orchestration on top of the data from Sitecore OrderCloud to align with Microsoft Retail industry standard schema.

While the key role of the connector is to get data into the retail data manager (RDM) schema, the primary benefit is the access you get to solutions that are built on top of RDM.

Currently, the following solutions can be enabled on top of Sitecore OrderCloud data:

  • Power BI reports on sales, products, and customer data: The Power BI reports come out of the box when you install Sitecore OrderCloud connector. The connector ensures that no personal information of users is transferred to enable the Power BI reports and other related solutions.
  • AI shopping assistant: AI shopping assistant is a starter app that lets consumers shop through an AI enabled chat experience. This app can be embedded into your own shopping websites and apps, which further help in enriching customer interaction in an organic way.
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts

results.Feature name: Use frequently bought together model
results.Business value:
The frequently bought together model is a data-driven tool that helps retailers make decisions on product placement and promotions based on insights on closely related products. It identifies the combinations of products that are most often bought together, revealing associated products that could be placed closer together to promote cross-selling activity and boost joint sales.

Retailers can compare frequently bought together revenues to measure the performance of promotion action, seasonality, and new shelf assortment by comparing two different time periods. They can also compare customer behavior by comparing different stores of different geography, population, and surface area, and product groups that drive more revenue by comparing items.
results.Feature details:
The frequently bought together model empowers the store or merchandising manager to make data-driven decisions on products’ placement and promotions. The frequently bought together model identifies the combinations of products that are most often bought together.

Upon deployment of frequently bought together, you find the following items in your Microsoft Fabric workspace:

  • Lakehouse with tables, which are a subset of the retail data model.
  • Prepopulated sample data in the Lakehouse tables for a fast-onboarding experience.
  • Notebook (name: Frequently bought together model).
  • Power BI report (name: Frequently bought together on retail data solutions report).

After you successfully deploy frequently bought together, you can configure the model to generate insights on the data available in the Lakehouse. The Microsoft Fabric notebook gives you the flexibility to execute the model in your own cadence, connect with input data anywhere in Microsoft Fabric, fine-tune the model parameters for specific customizations, and choose how to visualize the results.
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts

results.Feature name: Use retail industry data model
results.Business value: The retail industry data model offers substantial business value by addressing common challenges faced by retailers in managing and extracting insights from the vast amount of data generated throughout the retail lifecycle. By providing a structured framework for efficient data organization, the model overcomes issues such as data fragmentation, time-consuming retrieval, and inconsistencies, leading to enhanced decision-making processes. Its adaptability to changing business needs, along with features like guided user experiences and customization options, ensures retailers can tailor the model to meet specific requirements. This results in improved operational efficiency, effective business intelligence and analytics, contributing to data-driven strategic planning and business success.
results.Feature details:
The retail industry data model is a blueprint used by retailers to plan, architect, and design data solutions for data governance, reporting, business intelligence, and advanced analytics. This capability allows retailers to use the full or subset of the data model to meet their specific needs. Some of the key advantages of this model are:

  • Enables retailers to use a subset or full industry data model for retail to meet their specific needs.

  • Organizes different types of data generated within the retail ecosystem in a structured framework.

  • Defines relationships and rules governing the data leading to data consistency and accuracy, which helps in making key decisions.

  • Drives data simplification strategy across the organization by simplifying cross-application integration and workflows.

  • Provides effective analytics and business intelligence, which leads to the creation of meaningful reports, insights, and visualizations

  • Adapts easily to changing business needs by adjusting the data model for new data sources, processes, and technologies.

The retail data model is essential for creating a coherent and efficient system for managing and using data in the retail sector. It ensures the accuracy and consistency of data but also supports the strategic use of this information for informed decision-making and business success.

Features of retail industry data model:

  • Configure full or few entities from the retail database template through guided user experience.

  • Transform and store the data in the retail database template and view the data through the Microsoft Fabric interface.

  • Extend the standard entities that are deployed through the deployment experience to meet customer-specific needs.

  • Add/remove entities post-install based on the business need.
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts

results.Feature name: Empower associates to compare products for customers
results.Business value:
In today’s retail landscape, customer expectations are evolving rapidly. With a plethora of products available at retail stores, customers are overwhelmed with information and are looking for key points to help them make a buying decision. They look for meaningful interactions and expert advice throughout their shopping journey in physical stores. When customers want to know more about a product or compare two products in the same category, they reach out to store associates. Therefore, it's valuable for store associates to have access to product information and be able to help customers with the data points they are looking for.

This feature would enable store associates to interact with store operations agent using natural language inside Microsoft Teams. Store associates can ask the agent to compare products and highlight key aspects of the products, so they can easily assist the customers in their shopping decisions.
results.Feature details:
This feature in Store Operations agent uses Generative AI to empower retail associates in delivering exceptional customer service within physical stores. By leveraging connectors available in Copilot Studio, it seamlessly connects to data sources to fetch comprehensive product information.

Key highlights:

  • Pre-built topics: Pre-configure dedicated topics to use Copilot Studio actions, enabling the retrieval of required product details based on user input.
  • Generative AI: Highlight product features across user-specified attributes, presenting them in an easy-to-compare format.
  • Copilot Admins: View topic and action details in Copilot Studio and use the low-code platform to point actions to the correct data sources.
  • User experience: Access product information and compare products through their preferred user interface (Teams, web portal, etc.).
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts

results.Feature name: Use SAP connectors for inventory and order search
results.Business value:
Retail is a fast-paced environment. Store associates are trusted with managing store operations and also providing exception customer service. Among other queries, customers visiting retail stores interact with store associates to know about product availability and order status. At this time, they expect store associates to provide them with this information swiftly, so they can make an informed decision.

Typically, this product and order information is stored in an ERP system which retailers use, and in most cases, SAP forms the ERP backbone. Hence, it makes sense to enable the store associates to fetch inventory and order details from SAP without having to abandon the customer and go either to the POS terminal or the backroom to get this information. Customers are looking for information instantly. With this feature, store associates would be able to search for inventory and order status by interacting with Store Operations Agent through natural language and assist customers with their queries.
results.Feature details:
This feature leverages Generative AI to empower retail associates in delivering exceptional customer service within physical stores. By utilizing the Copilot Studio plugin architecture and SAP connectors available in Power Platform, it ensures seamless access to inventory and order details.

Key highlights:

  • Pre-built topics: A dedicated topic for checking inventory and order details is pre-configured to use Copilot Studio actions, enabling the retrieval of required information from SAP via connectors.
  • SAP ERP connector: Utilizes Remote Function Calls (RFC) to fetch necessary data from SAP.
  • Generative AI: Understands user intent to orchestrate the correct topic and provide relevant information.
  • Copilot Admins: Can view topic and action details in Copilot Studio, and use the low-code platform to point actions to the correct data sources (SAP).
  • User experience: Once configured, store associates can access inventory and order information through their preferred user interface (Teams, web portal, etc.).
  • Enhanced customer experience: Allows store associates to interact with the Store Operations Agent within their workflow, reducing customer wait times and enhancing the in-store experience.
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts

results.Feature name: Enable user feedback on agent responses
results.Business value: With Store Operations agent, retail frontline workers can now get answers to their queries on store policies, procedures, help documents, and more. They no longer need to wait for store managers to provide the answers, nor do they have to abandon customer conversations. While it's beneficial for store associates to get prompt responses, it's equally important for them to provide basic feedback on those responses. Feedback can include whether the response was helpful or not, was the information shared up-to-date or outdated, and overall, how users are finding the agent's assistance. This feedback allows Copilot Admins to review and update the knowledge sources so that store associates can provide better responses next time.
results.Feature details:
This feature in Store Operations agent leverages Copilot Studio capabilities to provide responses to user queries by accessing information from knowledge sources. It introduces a feedback mechanism to enhance user interaction and improve the quality of responses.

Key highlights:

  • Feedback logic: Users can provide feedback on agent responses through thumbs up or thumbs down, with an option to add additional comments.
  • Adaptive card submission: When the Feedback topic is enabled in Copilot Studio, store associates can submit their feedback via an adaptive card.
  • Dataverse integration: The Dataverse connector populates the feedback table with user responses.
  • Admin access: Admins can view user inputs and feedback in the Dataverse table and update knowledge sources accordingly.
  • User experience feedback: Users are also prompted to share their overall experience interacting with the agent, helping admins to further enhance the user experience.
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts

results.Feature name: Use Store Operations Assist admin in Microsoft Teams
results.Business value: Retail administrators and managers often deal with multiple applications that are meant to address specific operational areas across retail stores. Dealing with apps on diverse product lines and platforms creates additional overhead on their daily schedule and in their app experience. Retail administrators and managers can now access Store Operations Assist admin from within Microsoft Teams making the experience seamless.
results.Feature details: This feature enables retail managers to switch to Store Operations Assist admin from within Teams with just a single click on the Teams menu. The retail managers can stay connected to their Teams communications while at the same time effortlessly switch to and from Store Operations Assist to manage their daily activities or monitor and plan store operations through the embedded Power BI dashboard.
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts

results.Feature name: Allocate sustainability data
results.Business value: Allocate sustainability data as needed across various subsets such as products, production lines, and organizational units. Gain visibility into your high-impact products or areas and prioritize where to focus your resources on improvements.
results.Feature details: This feature allows you to visualize and track the relative impacts of your different offerings, such as products or brands, by enabling you to allocate your emissions as needed across your business. The new functionality allows you to allocate data at scale. With this feature, you can more flexibly define how you want to allocate your organization's emissions. You can view the allocated emissions in Power BI and Excel reports.
results.Enabled for: Admins, makers, marketers, or analysts, automatically

results.Feature name: Compare your sustainability progress with Copilot
results.Business value: Sustainability practitioners and chief sustainability officers (CSOs) will gain a better understanding of how your company compares to others in your industry. Discover what other companies are doing to accelerate their progress, how those initiatives are succeeding, and how their efforts are impacting their metrics.
results.Feature details: With the Sustainability Insights Copilot template, you will help sustainability practitioners and CSOs quickly assess how they compare to other companies in their industry. They can identify actions their competitors are taking and determine how those actions affect progress. They can use these learnings to build out new sustainability programs and initiatives.
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts

results.Feature name: Calculate product carbon footprints
results.Business value: Achieve greater alignment and efficiency by unifying your corporate emissions calculations with product footprint assessments. This integration streamlines data management, providing a holistic view of your environmental impact across both operational and product-level activities. It helps you make informed sustainability decisions and meet regulatory requirements more effectively.
results.Feature details: You can calculate the carbon footprint of your products by using bills of materials, emissions factors, associated emissions, and allocated emissions in Sustainability Manager. This feature allows you to compute product footprints, providing a comprehensive view of your products environmental impact. A key capability includes the Product footprint calculation. You can use your activity and emissions data within Microsoft Sustainability Manager to calculate the carbon footprint of your products. The calculation supports: -Bill of materials (BOM): Use the materials that make up a product to assess your individual and collective carbon impacts. -Emissions factors: Apply specific emissions factors to materials and activities to determine accurate carbon output. -Associated and allocated emissions: Include relevant emissions tied to the product, from production to distribution, to ensure a holistic footprint analysis. -Cradle-to-grave assessment: Calculate the total carbon footprint of a product from raw material extraction to end-of-life disposal, capturing the entire lifecycle impact.
results.Enabled for: Admins, makers, marketers, or analysts, automatically

results.Feature name: Manage ESG metric definitions
results.Business value: Easily select, understand, and compute relevant environmental, social, and governance (ESG) metrics. Customizable and extensible ESG metrics in Sustainability solutions in Microsoft Fabric help you with more informed decision-making, driving better business outcomes.
results.Feature details:
With this feature in sustainability data solutions in Microsoft Fabric, you can:

  • Discover prebuilt metric definitions and their use, including 37 prebuilt metric definitions for the Corporate Sustainability Reporting Directive (CSRD).
  • Select, compute, and store the metric output as a table.
  • Create custom metric definitions using a prebuilt metric definition function.
  • Visualize prebuilt metric output using the default Power BI dashboard.
results.Enabled for: Admins, makers, marketers, or analysts, automatically

results.Feature name: Trace data flow from input to final output
results.Business value: Microsoft Sustainability Manager lets you trace your data flow from input sources to final output. This data trail provides enhanced decision-making, compliance and accountability, increased efficiency and productivity, and a positive environmental impact. This feature benefits a wide range of users, including makers, business admins, and analysts, empowering them to make informed decisions based on a holistic view of their sustainability data. It provides added value by streamlining data access, improving transparency and traceability, and enabling better choices.
results.Feature details:
Follow your data's journey from the moment it enters Sustainability Manager to when it becomes the final output. This feature includes a unified report and Power BI dashboard for a straightforward view of your data's progression. You can also export a detailed report, which makes it easier to keep track of your data's history. The audit log is a key part of this feature, recording metadata on the latest changes to ensure compliance and help you make smart decisions.

On the technical side, this feature dynamically connects the Emissions and Activities tables, giving you a complete and detailed view of the data. With Power BI integration, you get flexible reporting tools and advanced visualization options, perfect for troubleshooting. Plus, you can create a preparation report with all the necessary fields from the Emissions and Activity tables, making your data analysis thorough and user-friendly.
results.Enabled for: Users, automatically

results.Feature name: Estimate a carbon fee based on EU’s CBAM
results.Business value: This Copilot Studio template aligns the carbon fee you set with the EU’s Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism (CBAM), which is currently in a transitional phase and expected to be fully in effect in January 2026. The goal of CBAM is to ensure that carbon pricing is factored into imported goods. These goods might be produced with less stringent emissions standards and can otherwise compete unfairly with EU goods that have carbon costs already built in. This feature helps you to support the EU's climate objectives, maintain competitiveness, and avoid potential regulatory penalties.
results.Feature details:
When you set a carbon fee, you can align it with the EU’s CBAM. The CBAM objective is to put a fair price on carbon-intensive goods imported into the EU to help ensure that the carbon prices of imports and domestic products align. This template helps you to confirm that a price is paid for the embedded carbon emissions of imported goods.

You can deploy the EU CBAM Estimator template from App Source or Power Catalog for use within Copilot Studio.
results.Enabled for: Users, automatically

results.Feature name: Retry failed activities in a calculation
results.Business value: This feature in Microsoft Sustainability Manager saves time and enhances productivity for sustainability managers, business analysts, and administrators who are responsible for accurate data reporting and analysis. It streamlines the process of recalculating activities that have previously failed, eliminating the need to rerun the entire calculation job when only some of the activities have failed to calculate.
results.Feature details:
This feature enhances the robustness of the system by addressing errors that occur during calculation processes. It aims to mitigate errors by automatically retrying system errors for failed activities at the end of a calculation job. It improves efficiency by also allowing you to update activity data and manually retry only the failed activities in a previously run calculation job.

This feature handles system errors with the following actions:

  • Automatic retries: The system automatically marks failed activities with data validation errors for retry. After the initial calculation job is completed, these activities are reprocessed.
  • Manual intervention: You can manually initiate a retry for failed activities inside the Calculation profile history by selecting the Retry button, which generates a new calculation job linked to the original job. This job focuses solely on the activities that encounter errors.
  • UI enhancements: The calculation job UI now includes a column for parent job lookup and retry jobs are clearly labeled with a Retry prefix for easy identification.
results.Enabled for: Users, automatically

results.Feature name: Calculate insurance emissions
