results.Business value: This feature simplifies the process of measuring and managing emissions from insurance activities. It aligns operations with green goals, demonstrating environmental commitment. Moreover, it ensures easy compliance with emissions reporting rules, reducing reputational risks. Developed with precision to meet Partnership for Carbon Accounting Financials (PCAF) standards, it offers structured methods for emissions calculation across commercial and motor lines, ensuring accuracy and comparability in disclosures. To make emissions calculation hassle-free, it includes pre-made models and templates, saving time and enabling seamless tracking and reporting of insurance-related emissions.
results.Feature details:
Simplify emissions measurement and management for insurance activities with our latest feature: Calculate scope 3 category 15: Investments - insurance-based emissions. Align with sustainability goals, ensure compliance, and reduce reputational risks effortlessly. Developed to meet PCAF standards, this feature offers structured calculation methods for commercial and motor lines, enhancing accuracy and comparability in disclosures. Pre-made models and templates streamline emissions calculations for transparency and easy reporting.

This feature provides comprehensive tracking and reporting of emissions associated with insurance activities. It utilizes the PCAF methodology to accurately measure emissions across the insurance value chain, considering factors such as underwriting, claims, and investment portfolios. By implementing this feature, you can gain better visibility into your indirect emissions from insurance-related activities. This feature helps you identify emission hotspots and enables targeted emission reduction strategies. It helps you meet sustainability goals, comply with reporting requirements, and enhance transparency by providing a standardized approach to quantifying and managing insurance-based emissions.
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts

results.Feature name: Comprehensively manage your energy portfolio
results.Business value: Understand your organization's energy profile across its lifecycle. Define your impact from energy generation, quantify your renewable energy portfolio, and make business decisions to streamline energy management for your portfolio.
results.Feature details:
Sustainability Manager helps you to comprehensively manage your organization's energy portfolio and focus specifically on energy-related metrics without conflating them with carbon-related data.

The Microsoft Cloud for Sustainability energy data model lets you import energy data into Sustainability Manager so you can report on all components of the energy lifecycle from generation to end use. Sustainability Manager provides a holistic representation of your organization's energy landscape separate from carbon data.
results.Enabled for: Users, automatically

results.Feature name: Disclose, analyze, and manage ESG data
results.Business value: Unify your environmental, social, and governance (ESG) data with sustainability data solutions in Microsoft Fabric. Prepare for sustainability, disclosures, analytics, and decision-making. This integrated approach helps you to drive sustainability initiatives, improve regulatory compliance, and meet your overall sustainability goals.
results.Feature details:
With sustainability data solutions in Microsoft Fabric, you can ingest, harmonize, and process ESG data from disparate sources. Specific sustainability scenarios include ESG metrics computation, Azure emissions reporting, and environmental analytics.

The capabilities include:

  • ESG data estate: This data estate allows you to combine ESG data from various sources into a standard schema that provides data models for environmental, social, and governance records. You can then use the standardized data to compute quantitative metrics that meet disclosure reporting requirements, such as Corporate Sustainability Reporting Directive (CSRD). You can also use aggregated datasets in analytics.
  • Microsoft Azure emissions insights: This data estate helps you to centralize, query, and analyze your Azure emissions data.
  • Environmental metrics and analytics: This data estate helps you to directly connect your environmental data from Microsoft Sustainability Manager to the data lake as tables. You can then process this data according to your sustainability needs to create custom environmental notebooks and build insights to better understand your carbon, water, and waste emissions.
  • Social and governance metrics and reports: This data estate allows you to combine disparate data from various sources into a standard schema that provides data models for social and governance records. You can then use the standardized data to define, compute, and analyze social and governance metrics. You can also visualize the trendlines and explore social and governance metrics through interactive dashboards.
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts

results.Feature name: Import and calculate with product carbon footprint data
results.Business value: Use product carbon footprint data to seamlessly calculate and understand supply chain emissions in Microsoft Sustainability Manager. Determine the greenhouse gas emissions that are associated with a product family. More easily import and manage this data within Sustainability Manager.
results.Feature details:
You can import product carbon footprint data into Sustainability Manager and view it on the Reference data page. Use product carbon footprint information to compute emissions in calculation models and view emissions data in terms of product within reports.

This feature includes updates to the user interface, calculation models, reports, and demo data to accommodate product carbon footprint data.
results.Enabled for: Users, automatically

results.Feature name: Resolve calculation errors faster
results.Business value: With enhanced error messaging for calculation jobs, you can swiftly identify and address issues, distinguishing between user errors and platform-related issues. This enhancement streamlines troubleshooting, enhances data accuracy, and aligns with sustainability goals, ultimately benefiting both users and the environment. These improvements aim to provide faster error diagnosis and resolution, ultimately leading to increased productivity and a better user experience within Sustainability Manager.
results.Feature details:
Enhanced error messaging for calculation jobs in Microsoft Sustainability Manager provides more informative and actionable guidance during calculation processes. The error messages are designed to be explicit and user-friendly, offering clear explanations of the cause and recommended actions for correction.

When encountering errors, the system presents precise error messages that help you understand the problem and, where possible, provide specific guidance on how to resolve it. For issues that require attention from the Sustainability Manager team, the error messages indicate that the problem isn't related to user input.
results.Enabled for: Users, automatically

results.Feature name: Load Defra 2024 emission factors
results.Business value: With the ability to load Defra 2024 factors in Microsoft Sustainability Manager, you can track and report greenhouse gas emissions and energy consumption in compliance with multiple compliance regulations. This feature simplifies the process of calculating and reporting emissions for different regulations, and it supports businesses in their efforts to reduce their environmental impact and align with sustainability goals.
results.Feature details: The Department for Environment, Food, and Rural Affairs (Defra) regularly updates emissions for use by UK and international organizations. You can download the latest 2024 Defra conversion factors to calculate greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions from a range of activities, including energy use, water consumption, waste disposal, and recycling/transport activities.
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts

results.Feature name: Load EPA 2024, Defra 2023, and NGER emission factors
results.Business value: With the ability to load EPA 2024, Defra 2023, and National Greenhouse and Energy Reporting (NGER) factors in Microsoft Sustainability Manager, you can track and report greenhouse gas emissions and energy consumption in compliance with multiple compliance regulations. This feature simplifies the process of calculating and reporting emissions for different regulations, and it supports businesses in their efforts to reduce their environmental impact and align with sustainability goals.
results.Feature details:
The Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) regularly updates emission factors based on scientific research and industry trends. You can download the latest 2024 EPA factors from the Microsoft Solution Center, ensuring accuracy and compliance with regulatory standards.

The Department for Environment, Food, & Rural Affairs (Defra) regularly updates emissions for use by UK and international organizations. You can download the latest 2023 Defra conversion factors to calculate greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions from a range of activities, including energy use, water consumption, waste disposal, and recycling/transport activities.

The NGER factor set is an additional set of factors that you can download from the Microsoft Cloud Solution Center to use for calculating emissions, specifically tailored for Australian operations. This feature supports the NGER scheme, which is a regulatory framework in Australia for reporting greenhouse gas emissions and energy consumption. By loading NGER factors into Sustainability Manager, you can:

  • Ensure compliance with Australian regulatory requirements, avoiding potential penalties.
  • Enhance accuracy in environmental reporting, leading to better sustainability management.
  • Facilitate strategic decision-making with reliable data on emissions and energy usage.
  • Demonstrate environmental responsibility to stakeholders and the public.
  • Drive efficiency in energy use, potentially leading to cost savings.
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts

results.Feature name: Load the 2024 NEEFE factors update
results.Business value: With the ability to load the 2024 National and European Emission Factors for Electricity (NEEFE) factors update in Microsoft Sustainability Manager, you can track and report greenhouse gas emissions using both the greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions using the activity-based approach of the Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change (IPCC) and the lifecycle (LC) approach.
results.Feature details:
With Sustainability Manager, you can effortlessly import emission factors from various authoritative sources, including the NEEFE. Sustainability Manager incorporates the 2024 NEEFE update to allow organizations and individuals to calculate greenhouse gas emissions using the activity-based IPCC and LC approaches.

The NEEFE factors are essential components for calculating and managing the environmental impact of electricity consumption within the European Union. They provide a standardized set of emission factors that can be used for obligatory reporting and risk assessment for financial institutions, as well as for calculating product carbon footprints (PCFs) and other environmental metrics.

The NEEFE factors library package will be available as an optional component when deploying Microsoft Sustainability Manager from the Solution Center. If you already have Microsoft Sustainability Manager installed, you can redeploy it from the Solution Center, selecting the NEEFE factors library package to load the factors into your existing solution.

For information about using emission factor sets, go to Configure emission factors.
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts

results.Feature name: Enter data more quickly with fewer required attributes
results.Business value: As a natural refinement of the MCFS data model and in response to customer requests, we have reviewed the data model and identified attributes that can be made optional. In some cases, they are completely optional, and in others, we introduced defaults as necessary.
results.Feature details:
We're introducing significant flexibility with the Sustainability data model by making previously mandatory attributes optional and providing default values. Data entry scenarios are simplified with the following changes:

  • Relaxed constraints on some required attributes when their impact on data quality and stability is determined to be insubstantial.

  • Default values where appropriate, ensuring that data remains visible and is categorized under "unspecified/other" when required for reporting purposes, such as when data quality type is unavailable.
results.Enabled for: Users, automatically

results.Feature name: Enable unrestricted country region codes
results.Business value: Increase accuracy and align with international standards with the ability to associate activities to a country/region and run calculations specific to that country/region in Microsoft Sustainability Manager.
results.Feature details:
This feature provides more flexibility to define and extend the set of countries in Sustainability Manager. You can specify any of the ISO 3166-1 standard formats during import. The standard formats include three variations:

  • Two-letter
  • Three-letter
  • Numeric

You can extend the default list with custom specifications to fit your organization's regional layout.

In Sustainability Manager, the country/region displays the name value irrespective of which format you used to import the country/region value.
results.Enabled for: Users, automatically

results.Feature name: Import energy data to accurately define energy profiles
results.Business value: Make data-driven decisions in Microsoft Sustainability Manager to optimize energy usage, reduce costs, and minimize environmental impact. Gain a holistic perspective of your organization's energy footprint, enabling seamless integration of data from various sources like utility bills, metering systems, and energy contracts, such as power purchase agreements (PPAs) and renewable energy certificates.
results.Feature details:
Facilitate comprehensive energy data ingestion in Sustainability Manager for all energy components, covering generation, procurement, renewable sources, and consumption.

Capabilities include:

  • Standardize data formats, units, and normalization factors to ensure consistency and facilitate accurate analysis and reporting across all energy components.
  • Track and report on renewable energy usage, including the ability to account for renewable energy certificates and other renewable energy attributes.
  • Ensure data accuracy, reliability, and completeness to support informed decision-making, regulatory compliance, and stakeholder reporting requirements.
  • Support organizations to set energy-specific goals, and performance tracking to help achieve their sustainability objectives and targets for all energy components.
  • Ensure that the capability is scalable and capable of handling large volumes of energy data, ensuring optimal performance as the organization grows and expands its sustainability initiatives.
results.Enabled for: Users, automatically

results.Feature name: Stage and manage sustainability data
results.Business value: This feature addresses one of the greatest challenges in sustainability reporting: data collection from disparate sources. Collect and organize your data into Microsoft Sustainability Manager from disparate sources and with different file formats. You can manage data collection throughout the entire process, enhancing the quality and reliability of your sustainability data.
results.Feature details:
Sustainability data is available across many parts of your organization. This feature allows sustainability practitioners to manage a collection of data from different responsible parties in your organization and manage the status of the request regularly until the data is finally integrated into Sustainability Manager.

With this feature, you can collect data from disparate sources and in different file formats and store it temporarily in a quarantined state until approval into Sustainability Manager. Data is requested, submitted, and undergoes verification resulting from multiple roundtrips of request-inspection-correction and resubmittal passes.

Data collection management includes:

  • Administration, including provisioning, data boundary management, and user management.
  • Data collection orchestration.
  • Collaboration and communications management.
  • Data inspection, verification, and approval flows within Sustainability Manager.
  • Association retention to original data owners and collection campaigns.
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts

results.Feature name: Export and import connections to other environments
results.Business value: This feature helps you adhere to recommended application lifecycle management (ALM) practices for Microsoft Sustainability Manager by letting you migrate your connections from a development to a test environment, or from either of those to your production environment.
results.Feature details: You can transfer connections from one Sustainability Management environment to another using ALM in Power Platform. With this update, you can test changes in your development and test environments and then propagate them to your production environment, helping you to avoid unforeseen human errors.
results.Enabled for: Admins, makers, marketers, or analysts, automatically

results.Feature name: Use IEA emission factors in Sustainability Manager
results.Business value: Use International Energy Agency (IEA) emission factors in Microsoft Sustainability Manager to accurately calculate emissions. Use IEA's emission factors to understand the carbon footprint of electricity generation, aiding in the development of strategies to reduce greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions. In addition, these calculations can assist with regulatory compliance, risk management, and cost reduction.
results.Feature details:
Sustainability Manager now supports the calculation of emissions using IEA emission factors. You can download them from the Microsoft Cloud Solution Center.

The IEA factor library is available to use alongside our other emission factor libraries in Sustainability Manager. However, there are some licensing restrictions. You can use the factor values in calculations, but you can't see them in the user interface. You can only see the values when you download them in a PDF file, and you can only see the specific factor values that you used in your calculations.

Using the IEA factor library includes the following steps:

  1. Ensure the country/region of interest is available in the library and create factor mappings, if needed.

  2. Create a calculation model and calculation profile that references the library and specific factor, and then run the calculation profile.

  3. Export the factors to PDF.
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts

results.Feature name: Set carbon fee in Sustainability Manager
results.Business value: Encourage your company to reduce its carbon footprint and become more environmentally sustainable. You can put an internal price on carbon emissions to incentivize your company or individual organizational units to find ways to reduce those emissions and save money.
results.Feature details:
Using the carbon fee feature in Microsoft Sustainability Manager, you can visualize and understand the costs of your organization’s emissions by scope and facility, country/region, or organizational unit. The fee is an internal company carbon fee that your company may choose to charge itself for each unit of carbon dioxide that it emits. You can set fees by scope or flat across scopes.

Calculated carbon fees are displayed in the Executive dashboard and Emissions insights dashboard. They include allocations by country/region, by organizational unit, and by facility. For example, if you select By organizational unit on the dashboard, you can see and compare the monetary impact of emissions across various organizational units. Use the insights to promote transparency around your internal costs of emissions and make more exacting decisions about reductions.
results.Enabled for: Users, automatically

results.Feature name: Enhance ESG insights with what if analysis
results.Business value: Enhancements to the existing what-if analysis feature in Microsoft Sustainability Manager helps your organization build a relationship between forecasting and reduction goal planning. You can link forecasts to existing goals to track actual progress alongside the projected one. In addition, forecasts with the same historical data can be layered onto a single view, allowing for faster analysis of optimal reduction opportunities.
results.Feature details: Take the guesswork out of the environmental and business impacts resulting from a change to your suppliers or practices. Use what-if analysis in Sustainability Manager to compare your organization's current carbon emissions trends with scenarios involving different variables in your business model. Visualize the potential impact of changing variables such as switching suppliers.
results.Enabled for: Users, automatically

results.Feature name: Estimate carbon fee with EU CBAM Copilot
results.Business value: This feature aligns the carbon fee you set with the EU’s Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism (CBAM), which is currently in a transitional phase and expected to be fully in effect in January 2026. The goal of CBAM is to ensure that carbon pricing is factored into imported goods. These goods might be produced with less stringent emissions standards and can otherwise compete unfairly with EU goods that have carbon costs already built in. This feature helps you to support the EU's climate objectives, maintain competitiveness, and avoid potential regulatory penalties.
results.Feature details: This feature aligns with CBAM, which has an objective to put a fair price on carbon intensive goods imported into the EU to help ensure that the carbon prices of imports and domestic products align. Using Copilot, this feature takes data provided by you or through Microsoft Sustainability Manager to estimate the carbon fee associated with importing goods into the EU. This feature helps your organizations plan for the budget required for these tariffs.
results.Enabled for: Users, automatically

results.Feature name: Connect and view ESG analytics from other sources
results.Business value: Use the ingestion capabilities of Sustainability data solutions in Fabric to efficiently bring in high-quality data at scale from outside sources. Lower the effort required to configure data ingestion for proprietary formats from popular enterprise systems. Reduce errors when mapping data between external applications and the analytical schema in Sustainability data solutions in Microsoft Fabric.
results.Feature details:
As a sustainability data engineer, you can use prebuilt connectors to easily ingest and standardize environmental, social, and governance (ESG) data from outside sources in Sustainability data solutions in Fabric. Manage and analyze ESG data effectively, ensuring that high-quality data is available in Sustainability data solutions in Fabric for future use.

The prebuilt connectors include:

  • Data pipelines
  • Notebooks
  • Utility functions

Use these connectors to select a list of tables from source systems and transform them into corresponding target tables in the ESG data model with notebooks in Sustainability data solutions in Fabric.
results.Enabled for: Admins, makers, marketers, or analysts, automatically

results.Feature name: Forecast emissions with what-if analysis
results.Business value: You can interact with an intelligent what-if analysis model in Microsoft Sustainability Manager to compare your current carbon emission trends against the forecasted carbon emissions impact of changing variables in your business model. Forecasting uses your organization's current practices, plus supplemental sustainability data, to intelligently predict the potential impact on your organization's carbon emission footprint over time. The what-if analysis model also simulates the impact of changes in product mix, planned production quantities, and improvements in short term water consumption efficiencies for products produced by your organization.
results.Feature details:
What-if analysis uses an intelligent model to forecast the carbon emissions impact over time of changing business practice variables.

You can view the analysis output within the Analytics area of Microsoft Sustainability Manager, or you can extract the data from Dataverse and extend it through custom Power BI reporting. The aim is to easily enable decision making across sustainability managers, procurement officers, facility managers, and any other users that want to leverage smart sustainability data to drive reductions in their sustainability impact.
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts

results.Feature name: Streamline ESG data collection with approvals
results.Business value: Data collection enables sustainability practitioners to efficiently obtain information from multiple stakeholders across the organization. By creating data plans or requests and consistently monitoring their progress, this capability ensures smooth integration of collected data into Sustainability Manager. This optimized approach enhances data accuracy, minimizes manual tasks, and boosts overall efficiency in ESG reporting.
results.Feature details:
Sustainability practitioners often face challenges when collecting ESG data from diverse sources, including databases and physical documents. Structured data can typically be integrated through data connectors; however, unstructured data frequently requires manual entry, a common practice in many organizations. In large companies, coordinating data collection efforts among numerous employees can be particularly difficult, especially if employees are unaware of the purpose or deadlines involved. This manual process is inefficient and time-consuming, underscoring the need for automated data collection workflows.

The data collection feature in Sustainability Manager helps practitioners efficiently gather data from various responsible parties within the organization. Practitioners can create data plans or requests and regularly track their status until the data is successfully integrated into Sustainability Manager. Recent enhancements, such as a multi-level approval mechanism, ensure improved data quality and accuracy. Additionally, a conversational agent simplifies the process for data owners to submit requested information. Practitioners can now also collect social and governance data alongside environmental data. These enhancements are automatically available in both the Essentials and Premium SKUs.
results.Enabled for: Users, automatically

results.Feature name: Improve data accuracy and completeness
results.Business value: Enhancements to data approval management in Microsoft Sustainability Manager provide additional capabilities to review, validate, and approve data. These capabilities ensure the accuracy and completeness of reported sustainability performance.
results.Feature details:
The data approval management feature provides a streamlined user experience with new capabilities. This streamlined experience provides a collection of enhancements, including:

  • Auto-approval rules.
  • Support for more entities.
  • Improved navigation with more intuitive filters and buttons.
  • Settings for group enable or disable, making it easier to enable or disable the feature across different product areas.
  • Multi-stage approvals.
results.Enabled for: Admins, makers, marketers, or analysts, automatically

results.Feature name: Build framework for third party connectors
results.Business value: Partners and vendors can use a Microsoft framework to build third-party connectors for partner solutions that allow customers to import data into Microsoft Sustainability Manager.
results.Feature details:
This framework and flow helps partners and vendors build third-party connectors to import data from partner solutions. This feature includes the template and complete documentation to help partners and vendors create third-party connectors for Microsoft Sustainability Manager.

Sustainability Manager provides several methods for data import, including a separate experience to import data from partners. We’re working with partners and vendors to build a robust catalog of prebuilt and custom connectors, and this feature provides the framework for partners and vendors to create these solutions.

To access new third-party connectors in Sustainability Manager, customers select the Partner Solutions tile under Data imports. They can then select the solution, enter their credentials, and then complete the guided steps to set up the import.
results.Enabled for: Admins, makers, marketers, or analysts, automatically

results.Feature name: Track your social and governance data
results.Business value: The Microsoft Cloud for Sustainability social and governance data model provides all the components you need for comprehensive ESG insights, enhancing decision-making, and supporting sustainable investment choices. These data model components provide a more complete snapshot of your company’s performance to support more sustainable investment decisions and help identify, analyze, prioritize, and guide decisions on various business risks. They also provide additional social and governance-related aspects that are critical to defining how your company operates, factoring in the planet and people.
results.Feature details:
You can get the Cloud for Sustainability social and governance data model in the Microsoft Cloud Solution Center. The social and governance tables/entities in this data model help you with:

  • Comprehensive ESG insights
  • Regulatory compliance
  • Critical social and governance aspects to guide company operations
  • Risk management
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts

results.Feature name: Bring Project ESG Lake data into Sustainability Manager
results.Business value: Use Project ESG Lake to calculate custom metrics with data from Microsoft Sustainability Manager. Then use this feature to move the results back to Sustainability Manager from Project ESG Lake.
results.Feature details:
This feature enables you to ingest data from Sustainability Manager into Project ESG Lake. There, you can calculate circularity metrics for carbon, water, and waste using custom logic. Finally, you can move the results back to the Sustainability Manager.

For example, if you want to calculate water mass balance using a custom formula, you perform the following steps:

  1. Ingest water data from Sustainability Manager into Project ESG Lake using pre-configured pipelines.
  2. Use notebooks to perform the custom calculations for water mass balance in ESG lake using the custom formula.
  3. Send the calculated metrics from Project ESG Lake back to Sustainability Manager, where they’re transformed into Dataverse with the Microsoft Cloud for Sustainability data model.
results.Enabled for: Admins, makers, marketers, or analysts, automatically

results.Feature name: Generate reports with external reporting
results.Business value:
External reporting enables your organization to collaboratively create voluntary and regulatory disclosure reports across a range of standards and frameworks. Approval and audit workflows help your organization ensure compliance and data accuracy.

With flexible collaboration and approval workflows plus the ability to organize and reuse disclosure data, external reporting reduces complexity, enhances efficiency, and provides greater transparency for ESG reporting.
results.Feature details:
ESG disclosure reporting can be a complex and challenging process for sustainability practitioners who need to report against numerous voluntary and regulatory reporting frameworks. Because these reports aren't standardized, it's difficult to know which metrics to report and how to structure disclosures. External reporting in Microsoft Sustainability Manager addresses these challenges by providing templates based on various ESG standards or frameworks. It helps you with the creation, completion, and approval of comprehensive reports across both quantitative and qualitative metrics.

Microsoft Sustainability Manager includes standard and framework reporting templates for:

  • Corporate Sustainability Reporting Directive (CSRD)
  • Australian Sustainability Reporting Standards (ASRS)
  • Business Responsibility and Sustainability Reporting (BRSR) 1 and 2
  • Global Reporting Initiative (GRI)
  • International Financial Reporting Standards (IFRS) 1 and 2
  • Various Sustainability Accounting Standards Board (SASB)

In addition, external reporting provides:

  • Extensibility for custom template creation.
  • Integration with MSM and SDSF data points.
  • XBRL framework compatibility, allowing you to create and reuse concepts, facts, and dimensions across reports.
  • Support for various response types for quantitative and qualitative disclosures including rich text, and numeric.
  • Ability to generate ESG disclosure response drafts with Copilot based on your documents.
  • Workflow UI for task creation and management and content approval.
  • Disclosure generation in Excel and HTML.
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts

results.Feature name: Report emissions for different regulatory standards
results.Business value: Report your organization's emissions in Microsoft Sustainability Manager using different calculations to meet the requirements of multiple regulatory standards. Produce parallel reports from the same original activities to satisfy multiple standards.
results.Feature details:
This capability helps you meet requirements from regulatory agencies that might have different regional or reporting standards variations.

To accomplish these goals, this feature provides these capabilities:

  • Run calculations on the same activities, producing emission records within independent and isolated groups.
  • Produce preparation reports independently for each emissions group.
  • Reports include an annotations section to describe produced emissions more elaborately.
  • Power BI analysis is based on different emission groups independent of each other.
  • Other analyses, such as what-if analysis and goals, are also based on independent emission groups.
results.Enabled for: Users, automatically

results.Feature name: Explore and plan data to meet CSRD requirements
results.Business value:
The European Commission has adopted a legislative proposal for a Corporate Sustainability Reporting Directive (CSRD). The directive requires companies within its scope to report on environmental, social, and governance (ESG) matters using a double materiality assessment in compliance with the European Sustainability Reporting Standards (ESRS) starting from reporting year 2024.

Thus, enterprise organizations need a mechanism to be on top of the ESG metrics inventory that needs to be reported. And as part of this effort, there is a need to understand and plan to get sustainability data from disparate systems (sustainability systems, HR, finance, CRMs, and more) for various metrics, transform the data for disclosures, draft qualitative declarations, link evidence to substantiate the disclosures, assess the disclosures by various internal auditors and finally generate the audited report in both human and machine-readable formats.

The CSRD preview solution enables you to explore and plan for the data required for CSRD. It also enables the workflow to collect the data for disclosures, internally review the disclosure data and generate a report for the reviewed disclosure data.
results.Feature details:
This feature provides the following enhancements to the CSRD reporting capabilities:

  • Streamlines ingestion and standardization of the data for the disclosure requirements.
  • Augments the evidence data to substantiate data for disclosures.
  • Streamlines audit and final report generation for sustainability regulatory reporting, starting with CSRD.

This feature also includes updates to the CSRD template (preview) in Purview Compliance Manager for the finalized reporting standards that the European Commission will publish over the next few months.
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts

results.Feature name: Compute and store ESG metrics for downstream scenarios
results.Business value: Data engineers and analysts can view prebuilt ESG metric definitions, select and compute metrics with ease, and store the results in a table for various downstream scenarios like visualization, reporting, or integration with ISVs. It also lets you add custom metrics or modify prebuilt ones, giving you the flexibility to address specific needs, such as country-specific sustainability reporting.
results.Feature details:
This feature helps you to:

  • Discover prebuilt metric definitions and their use.

  • Select, compute and store metrics' output using prebuilt pipelines and notebooks.

  • Create custom metric definitions using prebuilt utility functions.

  • Perform roll-ups across an organizational hierarchy and unit conversions on the data using prebuilt utility functions.

  • Visualize prebuilt metric output with the Power BI dashboard.
results.Enabled for: Admins, makers, marketers, or analysts, automatically

results.Feature name: Generate a CSRD preparation report
results.Business value: Organizations are faced with complexities in understanding the key requirements of the Corporate Sustainability Reporting Directive (CSRD). Understanding the requirements and data to include are paramount to a successful disclosure. After you understand the requirements, you need to map metrics or data points to these requirements. Gain enhanced efficiency and accuracy when disclosing CSRD requirements. You no longer have to sift through all your sustainability data to determine what to include to support the requirements defined within the CSRD. You can include the quantitative data captured through Microsoft Sustainability Manager in this updated assessment template.
results.Feature details:
You can find the CSRD preparation report in the Reporting area of Microsoft Sustainability Manager. To generate a CSRD preparation report, set the Report type to Corporate Sustainability Reporting Directive. Specify the report language, report name, reporting year, and organizational hierarchy information. Then you make sure your report includes the values you need by mapping your goals to each type CSRD goal.

You can map your goals to the following categories:

  • Scope 1
  • Scope 2 (location-based)
  • Scope 2 (market-based)
  • Scope 3
  • Total GHG emissions
  • Water management
  • Waste management
  • Air pollutants
  • Soil pollutants
  • Substances of concern or high concern

Your generated report includes quantitative and qualitative metrics and descriptions that appear in the European Sustainability Reporting Standards (ESRS).
results.Enabled for: Users, automatically

results.Feature name: Customize analytics reporting dashboards
results.Business value: Solving sustainability analytics needs requires a customization approach since many organizations have unique requirements related to content and layout. Organizations need the ability to create customized versions of the built-in insight pages across emissions, water, and waste. Analytical reporting extensibility in Microsoft Sustainability Manager empowers you to create a customized dashboard. Because the standard built-in insight pages are used as a baseline, there are significant cost and time savings in building a customized dashboard. The customized dashboard enables you to highlight content that's most important to you, supporting faster analysis and decision-making.
results.Feature details:
If you have a full access role in Sustainability Manager, you can copy any Emission, Water, or Waste dashboard. Sustainability Manager creates a copy of the built-in analytics report page, where you can:

  • Edit and delete existing analytical page components.
  • Add new metrics or components.
  • Change chart types.
  • Personalize the page layout.

After you custom tailor the analytical pages to your organizational requirements, simply save your changes, and the new dashboard is available to your users by default. You and your users can switch between the customized and standard dashboard with a new toggle.
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts

results.Feature name: Analyze your carbon data with intelligent insights
results.Business value: Strengthen confidence in your Microsoft Sustainability Manager data with intelligent insights. As a Sustainability Manager, you can view valuable insights about outliers, trends, and correlations in your emissions data. Our AI model helps you pinpoint and understand the key contributing factors for any outliers or trends you discover. Intelligent insights ensure informed decision-making and enhanced credibility by increasing the accuracy of your emissions reporting and helping you progress toward your carbon footprint goals.
results.Feature details:
Intelligent insights lower the error rate of your organization’s emissions reporting and provide valuable insights about how and when certain factors affect your carbon footprint. The insights surface outliers that can arise from errors in user input or measuring tools, helping you to avoid inaccurate reporting. You can also view trends and correlations between your activity data and emissions with our integrated and interactive AI model. These insights help you make more informed decisions about different practices across your business.

Intelligent insights provide the following functionalities:

  • A centralized location to view all your outlier, trend, and correlation insights.
  • Ability to sort and filter your insights so you can view only the ones you care about most.
  • Natural language expressions that provide a clear explanation of each insight.
  • Ability to bookmark insights that matter most to you.
  • Ability to select a day of the month on which new insights are generated on your data.
results.Enabled for: Users, automatically

results.Feature name: Increase CSRD coverage with added metric definitions
results.Business value: Use prebuilt metric definitions in Sustainability Data Solutions in Microsoft Fabric to compute CSRD quantitative metrics for downstream scenarios like environmental, social, and governance (ESG) reporting, audits, and visualizations. Reduce your time and costs for complying with disclosure standards.
results.Feature details:
This feature increases coverage of prebuilt ESG metrics in Sustainability data solutions in Fabric to cover all the remaining quantitative CSRD disclosure data points across European Sustainability Reporting Standards (ESRS):

  • ESRS 2
  • E1
  • E2
  • E3
  • E4
  • E5
  • S1

Sustainability data solutions in Fabric also maps the prebuilt metrics to the corresponding Global Reporting Initiative (GRI) disclosure data point where there’s an overlap between CSRD and GRI disclosures. With this feature, you can:

  • Deploy the ESG data estate capability updates to view the updated metrics library definitions and updated notebooks for generating required aggregate tables.

  • Use the existing pipelines and functions for generating aggregate and metric tables. After the metrics compute, you can visualize your data with a prebuilt dashboard.
results.Enabled for: Admins, makers, marketers, or analysts, automatically

results.Feature name: Use Copilot to generate facts for ESG reporting
results.Business value: Your organization faces a diverse and growing set of regulatory and voluntary frameworks, directives, and standards in addition to your own disclosure requirements. It's challenging to synthesize large amounts of data across your organization into a comprehensive response. Moreover, the continuous updating of environmental, social, and governance (ESG) regulations and guidelines require significant volumes of data to be processed and reported in multiple formats on an on-going basis. Copilot in external reporting (preview) helps you find ESG facts from your source documents and accelerates your ability to draft disclosure requirements.
results.Feature details:
Copilot in Sustainability Manager (preview) uses a large language model (LLM) to help you write qualitative and quantitative responses to fulfill ESG disclosure requirements. You can upload documentation and draft responses with references for various requirements across:

  • Corporate Sustainability Reporting Directive (CSRD)
  • Global Reporting Initiative (GRI)
  • International Financial Reporting Standards (IFRS)

Copilot saves you time and effort by finding the relevant data from your documents and suggesting how to write facts that meet the disclosure requirements. You can review and edit the facts before adding them to your reports.

Copilot in Sustainability Manager (preview) is enabled by admins in external reporting in Sustainability Manager. You can learn more about Project ESG Reporting (preview), including a discussion about facts, concepts, and other terms at Overview of Project ESG Reporting (preview), plus an in depth tutorial at [Tutorial: Overview of Project ESG Reporting (preview)](/industry/sustainability/project-esg-reporting tutorial-overview).

Learn more about Copilot in Sustainability Manager at Overview of Copilot in Microsoft Sustainability Manager.
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts

results.Feature name: Track sold products emissions for scope 3 category 11
results.Business value: Calculating emissions associated with the use of sold products as defined by scope 3 category 11 provides several benefits. These benefits include risk management by identifying potential climate-related risks, competitive advantage by developing low-carbon products, and improved supply chain management by working with suppliers to reduce emissions. By managing these emissions, your company can improve long-term profitability and sustainability. General availability reflects that this category completed the verification process. Organizations using Microsoft Sustainability Manager can confidently rely on verified categories for their sustainability assessments.
results.Feature details:
Sustainability Manager successfully completed the verification process for scope 3 category 11: Use of sold products. This category is now generally available, and the "Preview" label is removed from it throughout the product, including import, activity data, calculations, and documentation.

The verification process involved rigorous assessment and validation of data, methodologies, and calculations related to scope 3 category 11. Independent experts reviewed the accuracy, consistency, and compliance with industry standards. Their findings are on the Independent validation opinion page.

Sustainability Manager provides tools to track and report on emissions related to scope 3 category 11: Use of sold products. This category includes emissions that result from the use of goods and services sold by a company.

This feature includes the following key components:

  • Data model: The data model makes it easier to collect and organize activity data related to scope 3 category 11: Use of sold products.
  • Import: You can import activity data and calculate emissions for this category. You can still enter pre-calculated emissions if you prefer.
  • Default calculation models: Calculation models help translate activity data into emissions by leveraging our default factor libraries, including Environmental Protection Agency (EPA), Department for Environment, Food & Rural Affairs (Defra), or your own. This feature makes it easier for you to report on emissions and ensure accurate data.
  • Reporting: This feature will make it easier for sustainability managers to track and report on emissions from consumer use of sold products.
results.Enabled for: Admins, makers, marketers, or analysts, automatically

results.Feature name: Track investments emissions for scope 3 category 15
results.Business value: Financial institutions report emissions almost entirely in scope 3 category 15, emissions from investments. This category is essential to support the financial services sector. Calculating category 15 is complex and includes data from external sources and advanced allocation mechanisms. Microsoft Sustainability Manager has built a foundation that enables this engagement. This feature will utilize a solid methodology that can be widely used or modified to meet financial institutions' needs. General availability reflects that this category completed the verification process. Organizations using Microsoft Sustainability Manager can confidently rely on verified categories for their sustainability assessments.
results.Feature details:
Sustainability Manager successfully completed the verification process for scope 3 category 15: Investments emissions of the GHG Protocol. This category is now generally available, and the "Preview" label is removed from it throughout the product, including import, activity data, calculations, and documentation.

The verification process involved rigorous assessment and validation of data, methodologies, and calculations related to scope 3 category 15. Independent experts reviewed the accuracy, consistency, and compliance with industry standards. Their findings are on the Independent validation opinion. page.

Calculating scope 3 category 15 emissions includes an activity data model entity and new reference data to support the organizations' institutions. You can also import this data from various resources, internal and external. The calculation requires allocation after emissions have been calculated, and then attribution to outside organizations.
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts

results.Feature name: Track franchise emissions for scope 3 category 14
results.Business value: Franchise businesses can benefit from calculating scope 3 category 14 emissions by identifying opportunities to improve supply chain sustainability and reduce costs across all locations. Managing these emissions can improve brand reputation, customer loyalty, and regulatory compliance while attracting and retaining franchisees. General availability reflects that this category completed the verification process. Organizations using Microsoft Sustainability Manager can confidently rely on verified categories for their sustainability assessments.
results.Feature details:
Sustainability Manager successfully completed the verification process for scope 3 category 14: Franchise emissions of the GHG Protocol. This category is now generally available, and the "Preview" label is removed from it throughout the product, including import, activity data, calculations, and documentation.

The verification process involved rigorous assessment and validation of data, methodologies, and calculations related to scope 3 category 14. Independent experts reviewed the accuracy, consistency, and compliance with industry standards. Their findings are on the Independent validation opinion page.

Sustainability Manager provides tools to track and report on emissions related to scope 3 category 14: Franchises. This category refers to the greenhouse gas emissions generated by franchisees operating under a company's brand, as well as emissions from the supply chain associated with those franchisees.

This feature includes the following key components:

  • Data model: We've updated the data model to make it easier to collect and organize activity data related to scope 3 category 14: Franchises.
  • Import: You can import activity data and calculate emissions for this category. You can still enter pre-calculated emissions if you prefer.
  • Default calculation models: Calculation models help translate activity data into greenhouse gas emissions, making it easier for you to compute your carbon footprint, including franchisee emissions.
  • Reporting: This feature makes it easier for sustainability managers to track and report on greenhouse emissions across the supply chain.
results.Enabled for: Admins, makers, marketers, or analysts, automatically

results.Feature name: Track other fuel and energy for scope 3 category 3
results.Business value: Fuel and energy-related emissions go beyond direct emissions and energy purchases. Most organizations need to account for fuel and energy-related emissions in their value chain, called scope 3 emissions. These emissions include supplier-related activity and transportation and distribution losses of energy purchased. These emissions should be accounted for under scope 3 category 3: Fuel- and energy-related emissions not covered in scope 1 or scope 2. With the enablement of this scope 3 category, Microsoft Sustainability Manager provides the same simple access to accounting procedures used for fuel and energy, but with additional considerations needed within the value chain. General availability reflects that this category completed the verification process. Organizations using Microsoft Sustainability Manager can confidently rely on verified categories for their sustainability assessments.
results.Feature details:
Sustainability Manager successfully completed the verification process for scope 3 category 3: Fuel- and energy-related emissions not included in scope 1 or scope 2 of the GHG Protocol. This category is now generally available, and the "Preview" label is removed from it throughout the product, including import, activity data, calculations, and documentation.

The verification process involved rigorous assessment and validation of data, methodologies, and calculations related to scope 3 category 3. Independent experts reviewed the accuracy, consistency, and compliance with industry standards. Their findings are on the Independent validation opinion page.

This feature provides a data model and ingestion and calculation models for scope 3 category 3: Fuel- and energy-related activities not included in scope 1 or scope 2. It includes energy losses from transportation and distribution of purchased energy in scope 2, and any other utility or fuel information not covered by scope 1 or scope 2. Emissions from this category are aligned to the appropriate scope for reporting purposes.
results.Enabled for: Admins, makers, marketers, or analysts, automatically

results.Feature name: Track sold products processing for scope 3 category 10
results.Business value: Processing of sold products can have a significant environmental impact, particularly in industries where processing often involves energy-intensive activities such as heating, cooling, and refrigeration. By calculating emissions associated with this processing, your company can identify areas where you can reduce your environmental impact and work towards more sustainable practices. General availability reflects that this category completed the verification process. Organizations using Microsoft Sustainability Manager can confidently rely on verified categories for their sustainability assessments.
results.Feature details:
Sustainability Manager successfully completed the verification process for scope 3 category 10: Processing of sold products emissions of the GHG Protocol. This category is now generally available, and the "Preview" label is removed from it throughout the product, including import, activity data, calculations, and documentation.

The verification process involved rigorous assessment and validation of data, methodologies, and calculations related to scope 3 category 10. Independent experts reviewed the accuracy, consistency, and compliance with industry standards. Their findings are on the Independent validation opinion page.

Sustainability Manager provides tools to track and report on emissions related to scope 3 category 10: Processing of sold products. This category includes emissions generated from the processing of intermediate products that are sold by the reporting company but processed by third-party manufacturers before being used by the end consumer.

This feature includes the following key components:

  • Data model: The data model makes it easier to collect and organize activity data related to scope 3 category 10: Processing of sold products.
  • Import: You can import activity data and calculate emissions for this category. You can still enter pre-calculated emissions if you prefer.
  • Default calculation models: Calculation models help translate activity data into carbon emissions. This feature makes it easier for you to compute their carbon footprint for indirect emissions from third-party manufacturers.
  • Reporting: This feature makes it easier for sustainability managers to track and report on emissions across the supply chain.
results.Enabled for: Admins, makers, marketers, or analysts, automatically

results.Feature name: Use Citizen Services Agent in Copilot Studio
results.Business value: Navigating government services and information can be complex for citizens and can hinder their ability to access the services they need. An AI agent can significantly aid citizens in accessing this critical information and help facilitate public engagement.
results.Feature details:
Many public sector organizations make information about the services they provide available to their citizens. A citizen service agent can assist in finding relevant answers quickly with references to the sources, helping citizens find a specific government service, and guiding them on the next steps.

With this feature, Public Sector customers will be able quickly build their custom AI agent using their publicly available information to assist their citizens in finding answers and relevant sources. A public sector organization can get started with the Citizen Services agent in Copilot Studio as a starting point and easily configure and tailor it to their needs.

The agent is preconfigured with these capabilities:

  • Citizens can ask questions through natural language interface and expect to quickly get answers with links to the sources, based on the configured knowledge sources.
  • Citizens can ask about events, like road closures.
  • Citizens can submit requests to the organization, like applying for assistance.

For demonstration purposes, the agent comes preconfigured with publicly available websites as a knowledge source and API. Customers can customize this agent's topics and knowledge for their own needs.
results.Enabled for: Admins, makers, marketers, or analysts, automatically

results.Feature name: Use new Azure policy initiatives
results.Business value: Microsoft Cloud for Sovereignty policy portfolio, which expands on the Azure built-in policy initiatives, aids in customizing deployments to align with specific customer policy frameworks. These initiatives can reduce the time needed to audit environments and help meet established regulatory compliance frameworks and government requirements.
results.Feature details:
Microsoft Cloud for Sovereignty includes several policy initiatives to help public sector customers meet established regulatory frameworks. We're thrilled to announce the latest additions to Microsoft Cloud for Sovereignty policy portfolio. These updates will come throughout the upcoming release cycle as available.

In our continuous commitment to enhancing user experience, we're introducing the NIS2 policy initiative to help address various strategies for regions around the world. All the policy initiatives are available in our policy portfolio public GitHub as well as through Azure Policy as Built-In initiatives. This makes it easy to find and apply them to your Azure environments. These additions will further strengthen security and compliance measures for users operating in regions where these frameworks are important.

Furthermore, we're excited to begin working on supporting other regulated industries, starting with financial services. We are introducing the first baseline initiative to support our finance customers in their compliance goals. The baseline doesn't align with a specific framework but aims to provide a foundation for enhanced security.

We will also continue our collaboration with NATO on an Azure policy initiative for NATO's D32 directive on the protection of information in public cloud and expand our work into the NATO Restricted directive. This collaboration aims to review and validate the compliance of current cloud deployments with the D32 and enable continuous assurance. This extension underscores our commitment to supporting NATO's diverse security requirements and ensuring compliance across all levels of classification for the protected business network in a public cloud.

We continue to engage with our customers and build out these initiatives to help support a variety of use cases and compliance needs. These efforts aim to provide users with comprehensive compliance solutions tailored to diverse regulatory landscapes.
results.Enabled for: Admins, makers, marketers, or analysts, automatically

results.Feature name: Reviewing Azure services in Government Security Program
results.Business value: The availability of Azure services in the Government Security Program provides members with the ability to validate these services to meet their regulatory requirements for cloud adoption. The availability of these code kits enables customers to migrate their most sensitive workloads to the cloud.
results.Feature details:
Microsoft Cloud for Sovereignty has added information and artifacts to expand the GSP program to include Fabric Controller, Managed HSM, and Azure Virtual Desktop.

  • Fabric Controller: Customers can validate that VMs are isolated from all other users on physical hosts.
  • Managed HSM: Customers can validate the security of their keys in the event of hardware failure.
  • Azure Virtual Desktop: Customers can validate that their connection to their virtual desktop is secure regardless of the network environment.
results.Enabled for: Admins, makers, marketers, or analysts, automatically

results.Feature name: Digital sovereignty of Dynamics and Dataverse Services
results.Business value: Microsoft Cloud for Sovereignty offers customers a path forward to meet a single security baseline across multiple product lines. In our effort to enhance digital sovereignty across Microsoft services, including non-Azure services, we have published guidance on how to configure Power Platform and Dataverse environments. This will give you better control over your data, improving your digital sovereignty posture.
results.Feature details: Public sector and regulated industry customers of Microsoft's business applications often need to ensure sovereign control over their data and workloads. The published guidance for customers can help you enhance your data residency and access controls.
results.Enabled for: Admins, makers, marketers, or analysts, automatically

results.Feature name: Manage your cloud environment with new tools
results.Business value: Drift analysis enables customers to monitor how active cloud environments diverge from their desired architecture and configuration, identifying intentional or unintentional deviations. This provides a clear view of the environment’s evolution over time, allowing organizations to address any issues or risks that may arise. The assessment tool provides a pre-deployment evaluation of Azure resources against established best practices, including the evaluation of resource locations and Azure policy assignments.
results.Feature details:
We are making available on GitHub enhancements to our Lifecycle tools. In this release, we’re excited to introduce new capabilities designed to empower users with greater visibility, control, and compliance across their Azure environments. Our goal is to continually improve the efficiency, reliability, and compliance capabilities of our tools to better meet the evolving needs of our customers.

The Drift Analyzer and Assessment utility will be enhanced to enable users to save reports locally as well as view older saved reports, allowing for a more comprehensive view of Assessment and Drift status over time.

The Assessment utility will be adding a resource residency map. Once a user has run an Assessment, they will be able to visualize where all resources in their subscription are deployed using a map of Azure regions. This makes it easy to quickly see and understand where resources are deployed.
results.Enabled for: Admins, makers, marketers, or analysts, automatically

results.Feature name: Get customer guidance and reference for AI and LLM
results.Business value: Public sector organizations can take advantage of the latest AI innovation in the public cloud while managing their data in accordance with their local policies and regulatory requirements with the help of Microsoft Cloud for Sovereignty.
results.Feature details:
Cloud for Sovereignty introduces guidance and a reference architecture around sovereign AI and large language model (LLM) configurations.

This guidance includes an illustrative example of how to take advantage of LLMs and Azure OpenAI within the context of the retrieval augmented generation pattern. Specifically, this solution explores how these technologies can be applied within sovereign landing zones while considering important guardrails.

results.Feature name: Expand Sovereign policy portfolio
results.Business value: The Microsoft Cloud for Sovereignty policy portfolio, which expands on the Azure built-in initiatives, aids in customizing deployments. It reduces the time needed to audit environments and helps meet established regulatory compliance frameworks and government requirements.
results.Feature details:
Microsoft Cloud for Sovereignty policy portfolio includes several policy initiatives to help public sector customers in their efforts to meet established regulatory frameworks. We're thrilled to announce the latest Microsoft Cloud for Sovereignty Policy Portfolio additions.

In our continuous commitment to enhancing user experience, we have introduced the Spain ENS initiative to our policy portfolio public GitHub as well as through a built-in Azure Policy. Additionally, we continue to enhance and update existing initiatives as regulatory frameworks and guidelines are updated.

In addition, we have also introduced a policy initiative to verify compliance against the NIS2 directive. We continue to collaborate with NATO to not only improve the D32 directive policy initiative but also extend its scope to include the handling of NATO-restricted information. This extension underscores our commitment to supporting NATO's diverse security requirements and ensuring compliance across all classification levels for the protected business network in a public cloud.

Lastly, we're dedicated to broadening support for additional regulatory frameworks, particularly focusing on multinational requirements in Europe as well as other global regions. These efforts aim to provide users with comprehensive compliance solutions tailored to diverse regulatory landscapes.

results.Feature name: Use Terraform to manage Sovereign Landing Zones
results.Business value: The Sovereign Landing Zone (SLZ) helps organizations with advanced sovereignty needs meet their regulatory compliance requirements through Terraform, the industry-standard multi-cloud tool for Infrastructure-as-Code (IaC) and Policy-as-Code (PaC) capabilities. Customers can now use Terraform to configure and deploy their landing zones and enforce compliance.
results.Feature details:
What’s New: In this release, the Sovereign Landing Zone (SLZ) can now be deployed using both Terraform and Bicep. The Terraform version of the SLZ has the same structure, functionality, and configurability as the Bicep version. It also provides the same compliance posture over the resources deployed.

Key highlights:

  • Compliance & sovereignty: Both Terraform and Bicep-based SLZs maintain robust compliance and sovereignty posture enforced through Azure Policy.
  • Infrastructure parity: Common core Azure resources, such as hub networking, are available in both Terraform and Bicep versions.
  • Foundation: The Terraform-based SLZ is built on the new Azure Verified Modules (AVMs), which ensure top-tier support.
  • Interoperability: The modularity of the Terraform-based SLZ enables organizations with prior Terraform investments to supplement the SLZ with their existing modules.

This update ensures a seamless and flexible deployment experience while maintaining the highest standards of compliance and support, thereby enabling organizations with existing Terraform expertise fully leverage these investments to address their sovereignty needs.
results.Enabled for: Admins, makers, marketers, or analysts, automatically

results.Feature name: Improve Sovereign Landing Zone network configurations
results.Business value: The Sovereign Landing Zone enables organizations with advanced sovereignty needs to help meet their regulatory compliance requirements through Bicep as the Azure-native tool for Infrastructure-as-Code (IaC) and Policy-as-Code (PaC) capabilities in a configurable manner that can enforce resources to be in a compliant state.
results.Feature details:
In this release wave, the Sovereign Landing Zone through Bicep will deliver continued improvements for new and existing Sovereign Landing Zone deployments, including:

  • Enabling more configuration options for networking resources, allowing organizations to fully own the resources' lifecycles.
  • Supporting bring-your-own scenarios for existing network resources.
  • Enabling all capabilities provided in the latest version of Azure Landing Zone.

results.Feature name: New capabilities of the Sovereign Landing Zone
results.Business value: These enhancements to the Sovereign Policy Baseline help organizations ensure that only verified boot loaders, OS kernels, and drivers are used by Azure VMs.
results.Feature details:
The new capabilities of the Sovereign Policy Baseline include:

  • Support for auditing Azure VM SKUs that do not support Trusted Launch capabilities.
  • Support for auditing Azure VMs configured to not use Trusted Launch capabilities, such as allowing unverified boot loaders.

results.Feature name: Deploy RAG AI securely with Information Assistant
results.Business value: Agent templates will help public sector and regulated industry customers rapidly take advantage of the latest AI innovations in the public Azure cloud.
results.Feature details:
The Information Assistant agent template provides a starting point for organizations to build their own custom generative AI capability to extend the power of Azure OpenAI to organizational users and their private domain data. The agent template is a combination of code, documentation, and educational resources provided at no charge to customers and partners that can help accelerate time to value.

The next release will continue the evolution of Information Assistant secure mode, with improvements to document processing based on Azure AI Search pipelines to harness the growing features of Azure AI Search, and accessibility improvements.

results.Feature name: Enable agriculture LLM plugin
results.Business value: By harnessing the power of Azure OpenAI service, we’re enabling large language model (LLM) capability for agriculture that offers valuable insights to customers that are based on satellite, weather, sensor, farm equipment and hierarchical data in customers instance of Azure Data Manager for Agriculture (ADMA). This presents an effortless way for customers and partners to query and get insights from agronomic data in natural language.
results.Feature details: The integration of artificial intelligence (AI) and machine learning (ML) in various industries has brought transformative changes, enabling increased efficiency and productivity. One such domain that can benefit from AI is agriculture. By harnessing the power of Azure OpenAI service, we can create an LLM plugin for agriculture, offering farming context, valuable insights, and recommendations to customers in natural language. Our challenge is using data ingested and organized in ADMA to present an agronomy context for an area of interest for our customers. Our B2B customers can take the context from ADMA, add their own knowledge base, and get summaries, insights, and answers to their data questions through ADMA LLM plugin using natural language.
results.Enabled for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts

results.Feature name: Gain insights from ADMA with Copilot in Agriculture
results.Business value: By harnessing the power of Microsoft Fabric copilots, we’re enabling an LLM capability for agriculture that offers valuable insights to customers that are based on satellite, weather, sensor, farm equipment and hierarchical data in customers instance of Azure Data Manager for Agriculture (ADMA). This presents an effortless way for customers and partners to query and get insights from agronomic data in natural language.
results.Feature details: The integration of artificial intelligence (AI) and machine learning (ML) in various industries has brought transformative changes, enabling increased efficiency and productivity. One such domain that can benefit from AI is agriculture. By harnessing the power of Microsoft Fabric copilots, we can create an LLM plugin for agriculture, offering farming context, valuable insights, and recommendations to customers in natural language. Using data ingested and organized in ADMA we will provide agronomy context for an area of interest for our customers. Our B2B customers can take the context from ADMA, add their own knowledge base, and get summaries, insights, and answers to their data questions through ADMA LLM plugin using copilots in Microsoft Fabric.
